Â Â Regina Doherty (PPE). â MrÂ President, Commissioner, I think we can all agree that free and open trade is good for everyone. It's the best way to make sure that we spread wealth and prosperity. So, it stands to reason that tariffs are bad for everybody and tariffs are going to make all our countries poorer.

I'm glad that since the beginning of this ludicrous oneâman trade war, the EU and you particularly, have stood consistently as the adult in the room, because we all should be clear: in all of our Member States, ordinary working people are looking on and are in fear of losing their jobs and in economies faltering.

What we must do is we must protect our agrifood and our drinks industries, our pharmaceutical companies, our heavy goods manufacturing. There are so many other sectors where tariffs â even 10Â % â will result in job losses.

A reprieve till 1Â August is a good move, but what we need is a resolution. We should not be talking about retaliation at this point. We should be talking about a pragmatic solution and European leading the way.

So, I want to just wish you the very good, to assure you that you we all stand behind you and your team, and we're very proud of the practical approach that you have taken in the last number of months.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Maria Grapini (S&D). â Domnule preÈedinte, domnule comisar, negocierea comercialÄ Ã®ntre Uniunea EuropeanÄ Èi Statele Unite este vitalÄ, dar trebuie sÄ recunoaÈtem cÄ au trecut opt luni de la alegerea preÈedintelui Trump Èi nu Ã®naintÄm.

DumneavoastrÄ aÈi spus, Ã®ntre altele, cÄ âÃ®n curÃ¢ndâ. AÈi folosit aceastÄ sintagmÄ: cÄ vom avea textul Ã®n curÃ¢nd. Ne puteÈi spune mai mult ce cuprinde acest text? Care vor fi pÃ¢nÄ la urmÄ negocierile? Pentru cÄ producÄtorii din Uniunea EuropeanÄ nu pot sÄ stea pe loc. Din Èara mea, avem export de vin, avem export de produse agricole. Ce fac? Ei nu au curajul sÄ mai producÄ, sÄ mai investeascÄ pentru acea capacitate.

Èi apoi aÈi vorbit de acorduri cu alte state. De acord. VreÈi sÄ faceÈi acord cu India. Acordurile comerciale pot sÄ submineze producÈia industrialÄ. Èi vÄ dau un exemplu: plÄcile ceramice din RomÃ¢nia, Polonia, FranÈa sunt acum la pÄmÃ¢nt. Industria aceasta este la pÄmÃ¢nt pentru cÄ au intrat din India plÄci ceramice cu preÈuri la jumÄtate. Pentru cÄ India, Ètim bine, e cel mai mare poluator. Nu respectÄ nimic, nu plÄteÈte nimic.

Deci cum sÄ facem sÄ nu subminÄm industria europeanÄ?

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Sebastian Tynkkynen (ECR). â MrÂ President, even an imperfect deal is better than no deal at all.

What do we have to gain by not fully trying to uphold the transatlantic partnership?Â The US Government has made it clear that being bystanders and free riders is not an option. This includes not only defence, but also economy.

How can we ever reach a zero-tariff agreement if we are not ready to work for it?Â Europe is eyeing a critical choice: where to go to replace Russian energy and Chinese imports?

Make no mistake: if the problem lies in the East, then the solution must be found in the West.Â We have an opportunity to make the Atlantic the most prosperous economic zone of our time.Â So letâs not throw this chance away just for petty political battles.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Oihane Agirregoitia MartÃ­nez (Renew). â SeÃ±or presidente, estos son los aranceles con que nos amenaza Donald Trump: el generalizado del 10Â %, 25Â % a la automociÃ³n, 50Â % al acero y aluminio, amenazas del 17Â % a productos agroalimentarios, anuncios del 50Â % al cobre, el 200Â % a productos farmacÃ©uticos... Hoy vencÃ­a esa amenaza. Ahora la siguiente fecha es el 1 de agosto y estamos a la espera de unas cartas que ya han recibido algunos paÃ­ses como Corea del Sur o JapÃ³n.

Donald Trump dice que recibir una carta significa un trato. Yo creo que tenemos que decir claramente que una carta con una decisiÃ³n sobre un arancel es una acciÃ³n unilateral con toque dictatorial.

Y, desde luego, Donald Trump tampoco deberÃ­a condicionar con quiÃ©nes nos relacionamos o no, como es el caso de los BRICS, de los que forman parte paÃ­ses con los que yo, desde luego, no tengo ningÃºn interÃ©s en tener fuertes relaciones; pero ningunear a quienes suponen la mitad de la poblaciÃ³n y el 40Â % del PIB mundial es otra evidencia mÃ¡s de su despotismo.

OjalÃ¡ lleguemos a un acuerdo que sea beneficioso, pero, si no es asÃ­, les pedimos que sea la UniÃ³n Europea quien ponga plazo lÃ­mite y active el plan B.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Le PrÃ©sident. â La procÃ©dure Â«catch the eyeÂ» est close.

Pour les autres collÃ¨gues, il vous est possible de faire une dÃ©claration Ã©crite, qui sera inscrite au procÃ¨s-verbal inÂ extenso de notre sÃ©ance.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

(Fin des interventions Ã  la demande)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â MaroÅ¡ Å efÄoviÄ, Member of the Commission. â MrÂ President, MadamÂ Minister, honourable Members, first and foremost, I would like to thank you for all your interventions in what was, I'm sure, in most cases, well-meant advice. But at the same time, I would like to tell you that these negotiations are like no other. From the first moment, the Commission and myself have been doing exactly as you ask us to do â to focus on negotiated solutions and be prepared for any eventuality. This is the case from day one. This is the case today.

You probably know me by now and, in especially such a delicate negotiation situation as we have right now, I prefer good results to strong statements or tough headlines. Because I know how all this we are discussing with our American partners would affect jobs, how it would affect the economy, and how trade is important for the economic future of the European Union.

Some of you have been referring to industries. I can tell you that I spend an enormous amount of time talking to the industries which are most impacted by this transatlantic tension. I can tell you that 99Â % of them are calling for negotiated solutions. They've been built over the decades as transatlantic supply chains, and they do not want to be caught in the crossfire of ever-growing tariffs from both sides. So I think that we have to invest as much as possible in negotiations because the other alternative is always there. I can tell you that I will pursue it to the last possible moment and try to do my best to land this deal and to get the best possible result.

Many of you have been highlighting â and I really would like to thank you for that â the importance of the trade diversification and other agreements. We've been discussing it at different venues, but a quick recapitulation because there I also would need your help. We concluded our negotiations with Mercosur, with Mexico. We are very close to concluding with Indonesia. We are working very hard with India and the United Arab Emirates, and I believe that we can conclude the negotiations this year. We are continuing negotiations with Thailand, Philippines, Malaysia and re-engaging with Australia.

But I would need your help in presenting these deals in positive terms. I would need your help to accelerate the approval procedures. I would need your help to talk to your domestic audiences back in your Member States about the need to ratify these agreements, because otherwise they will just stay on paper and we are losing precious time from a long pause between when they are negotiated and when actually they enter into force. I can tell you that I have the same message and I'm repeating it at every single trade ministerial Council. I'm glad that more and more colleagues are understanding this from the same perspective.

If you allow me one thought on our farming community. As you know, I was in intense discussions with our farmers in my previous mandate. I know how they feel. I know what they are worried about. I know how they've been overburdened by excessive reporting, and how they've been afraid about the possible trade deals, which might negatively impact their wellbeing. Therefore, we are extremely careful when we negotiate agricultural chapters. Therefore, we are looking for the deals where the partners are net importers of our agri-food products, like Mexico, like the UAE. I think if you travel to these countries, just go to the store and you will see that these are usually European brands, with most of the agri-food products coming from Europe.

We are, in fact, the global superpower in exporting agri-food products. More than EURÂ 230 billion with a positive balance of EURÂ 70 billion. And we want to make sure that it would stay like that. But I would really resent the picture that every trade deal we do is at the sacrifice of our farmers because it's not true. I'm ready to engage in any discussions, together with Agriculture Commissioner Christophe Hansen, who is doing a fantastic job in being in touch with the farming community and is helping me with negotiating these trade deals, because we want to have a fair deal for everyone.

In the current turbulent situation in the global trading sphere, it's very clear that we need new market access, and we need it now, because the European Union economy is deriving half of its GDP from trading. So please help me to make it faster, to make it better, and to deliver better perception of our trade deals with your constituencies, with your voters, because you are influential people and you have that authority and they will believe you.

If you allow me a last point, because I know how important it is for MrÂ Warborn and for all the colleagues from INTA: WTO. I don't think I am exaggerating when I can tell you that I have an excellent personal and professional relationship with DrÂ Ngozi Okonjo-Iweala, Director General of the WTO. We've been together in many multilateral sessions, and she knows that Europe is the number one supporter of a reformed WTO. But reformed it must be. We had a WTO session at the OECD meeting in Paris, and there was not a single minister who said that everything is fine and rosy. Every single one was saying we need to reform.

The measures we wanted to take, for example, for e-commerce, for fisheries, for dispute settlement mechanisms are long overdue. I think we have to help the WTO, we have to help the Director General to reform this organisation, and I think the clock is really, really ticking. And the next ministerial in Cameroon in the spring of 2026 would be absolutely crucial in in that regard. So I can assure you that the WTO, even in the current state, with all the issues which are there, we consider as an absolute, basic, necessary platform for global trading practices because these are the rules we built over 70-80 years since the Second World War. So I can assure you that I will always be championing a functional, modernised WTO and that we will do our utmost to make sure that the reforms which are necessary will be really done.

Thank you very much for this debate. I know how tense it is. I know how important it is. I can only promise you that we will work until the last minute, when we see that it's possible to land a deal, because I think that's our duty and the obligation.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marie Bjerre, President-in-Office of the Council. â MrÂ President, honourable Members, Commissioner, thank you again for inviting the Council to this debate.

As many of you said â in particular MrÂ Warborn from EPP â it is important that we do not escalate the situation. The US is our strategic partner and ally. Our trade and investment relationship remains of key importance. But also, as MsÂ Karlsbro from Renew said, Europe is also important for the US. That is why we remain committed to finding a fair and mutually beneficial negotiated solution.

At the same time, we need to focus on ourselves â what we can do to help our industry to enhance competitiveness. What we can do to strengthen our internal market. What we can do to enhance our competitiveness and how we can ensure economic growth. Also, we have to focus on making trade agreements with other countries, as several of you mentioned today.

I also listened very closely to your comments about not breaking WTO, that trade has to be predictable, that the EU also should not be dictated to, that we should not give in and that we should remain firm. As MsÂ Van Brempt from S&D, MsÂ Hahn from Renew and MrÂ Lange from S&D very clearly said, we should not accept a deal at any cost. We want a deal and we are ready for a deal, but not at any cost.

If the United States is serious about partnership, the next days must be used wisely. We trust the European Commission to lead these negotiations on behalf of the Union with determination and unity, and we fully support its efforts to reach a fair and mutually beneficial solution.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Le PrÃ©sident. â Le dÃ©bat est clos.

DÃ©clarations Ã©crites (article 178)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 		Â 

Â Â Vytenis Povilas Andriukaitis (S&D), in writing. â Dear colleagues, dear Commissioner,

The EU is a very strong trade power globally. We have a lot of trade agreements with many countries, world-wide.

Trump is arrogant and unpredictable, and it is his weakness, but you, commissioner, are reliable and experienced, and that is your strength. We trust you.

A deal is possible, the EU goal is to reintroduce tariff levels, explore commercial opportunities, but we canât accept any negotiations on our legislation, on our high food safety standards and on our issues related to digital technologies.

Our strength is âreciprocalâ tariffs, WTO rules and our trade agreements, with new global partners.

Â 

12. EU Preparedness Union in light of the upcoming wildfire and droughts season (debate)
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â Le PrÃ©sident. â Lâordre du jour appelle Ã  prÃ©sent le dÃ©bat sur la dÃ©claration du Conseil et de la Commission intitulÃ©e Â«LâUnion de la prÃ©paration dans la perspective de la prochaine saison des feux de forÃªts et des sÃ©cheressesÂ» [2025/2771(RSP)].

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marie Bjerre, President-in-Office of the Council. â MrÂ President, honourable Members, Commissioner, strengthening preparedness and crisis management at EU level is a political priority for the European Union. Our leaders have repeatedly underlined the importance of a comprehensive and coordinated approach, and called for further actions in this area. The new preparedness Union strategy by the Commission and the High Representative in March this year must be seen in this context.

Europe needs to move from a reactive approach to a 'preparedness by design' approach â one that anticipates, prevents and effectively responds to a wide range of crises. The extreme weather events, the COVIDâ19 pandemic, Russia's aggression against Ukraine and hybrid attacks underscore the risk and threats the EU faces and will continue to face.

As further challenges are expected to become more frequent, complex and interconnected, we need to move to an approach that enhances resilience across the EU.Â Effective preparedness demands engagement at every level of society. It is primarily a national competence, but there is certainly room for more collective action at the EU level.

The Danish Presidency considers preparedness, resilience and crisis management a priority. A number of the policy actions announced in the Union strategy will be presented by the Commission during our presidency and we will examine them carefully in the Council.

In particular, we will focus on the legislative proposal on a revision of the Union Civil Protection Mechanism, which we expect shortly. We will work towards a negotiating mandate with a view to the negotiation with the European Parliament.

The Union Civil Protection Mechanism is certainly an EU success story. It coordinates the delivery of support from Member States or EU capabilities, such as rescEU, to a Member State that is overwhelmed.Â Since the beginning, the mechanism has helped so many communities, including those hit by forest fires. That is why the revision of the mechanism needs to build on this success and to make sure that it continues to deliver the successful cooperation it does today.

As we consider these policy initiatives, we must recognise that immediate and decisive action is critical given the worsening of the extreme weather events as recognised in the Union strategy.

In recent days, we again have seen the devastating impacts of climate change, with many areas of Europe suffering the effects of extreme heat. Once again this summer, we are expecting large wildfires and long droughts in various parts of our continent. These events are becoming more and more frequent and more extreme, and they pose an unprecedented threat to our populations, to our economies and to our ecosystems.

As the continent experiences the fastest rate of heating globally, you must get better prepared and quickly. Mitigation and adaptation measures to counter climate change are crucial. As a presidency, we work for a secure, competitive and green Europe. That is one of the reasons why the green transition is essential for a secure and competitive Europe, and to counter the effects of climate change.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

PRESIDENZA: ANTONELLA SBERNA
Vicepresidente

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Hadja Lahbib, Member of the Commission. â Madam President, honourable Members, today, our European continent faces record heat, prolonged drought and devastating wildfires. In just the past ten days, thousands of hectares have burned in France, in Greece, Germany, Spain, Albania, Serbia and Turkey. We are witnessing the destruction of ecosystems that will take years, even decades to recover, and our citizens are feeling the impact of this extreme weather in their homes, their health, and their livelihoods.

These are not distant warnings. They are the direct consequence of climate change, and they are sending us a very clear message. In the latest Eurobarometer on climate change, nearly four out of ten Europeans say they feel personally exposed to environmental and climate risks. And over eight out of ten believe that better preparation for climate impacts would improve their daily life. So we need to listen to them.

Honourable Members, we cannot look the other way. The heat wave many of us experienced just ten days ago is a stark reminderÂ â the climate is changing right before our eyes. Droughts and wildfires are closely connected. Dry conditions make fires more likely and far more dangerous as well.

All signs point to a difficult wildfire season this year. Many regions are already facing severe drought. Wildfire activity has been above average since the start of this year, and this summer extreme heat is forecast across most of Europe and beyond.

My thoughts today are with the citizens who fear for their lives, who have lost their properties due to the devastating wildfires around Narbonne and in Marseille. And of course, I would like to express my gratitude and my admiration for the rescuers who risked their lives fighting these fires.

But now the EU is better prepared. This year again, the European Union stands ready to support Member States and for any country that requests our help, our teams are on standby 24/7. While national authorities lead our wildfire response, we are working side by side with them every step of the way, and the EU can step in even before wildfire starts. We are using science and technology to monitor droughts and detect fires at a very early stage. This includes the use of drones and AI to detect and respond to fires. Our systems send real time alerts across Europe.

Our Copernicus satellite system provides free, high-resolution maps of fireâaffected areas and their surroundings and Member States are using it. Just recently, Greece requests Copernicus imagery to monitor wildfires in on Chios island and earlier this month in Crete. In past years, we have seen moments when Member States simply could not help each other because they were all facing high wildfire risks at the same time.

In 2017, Europe experienced its worst wildfire season in history. More than 130 lives lost and around 1 million hectares burned. That was a turning point. The EU responded by strengthening the European Civil Protection Mechanism and creating rescEU, a strategic reserve of firefighting planes and helicopters to ensure that no Member State is ever left alone to face devastating wildfires.

Honourable Members, this summer we have 22 firefighting planes and four helicopters ready to respond. They are stationed in ten countries strategically prepositioned to cover the highest risk areas. That's how two Greek Canadairs were deployed, for instance, to Albania within hours, three days ago. In May, I visited the TorrejÃ³n air base in Spain, which hosts several Spanish and rescEU Canadairs. I was deeply impressed by the professionalism and the dedication of the air crews, working under extremely tough conditions, both in the air and also at sea. And it's not just in the skies. On the ground, 25 fighting teams from across Europe are ready to step in wherever they are needed. Between July and September, we have also strategically pre-positioned around 650 firefighters from 14 countries in wildfireâprone areas, including Greece, Portugal, Spain and France over the summer. We started this prepositioning as a pilot in 2022 with 236 firefighters from six countries. We are now at 650 from 14 countries developed in four countries. So next week I will visit, for instance, Czech team stationed in Greece this summer. Their presence on the ground means faster response times. It also creates opportunities for learning and cooperation between teams from across Europe to build trust among them.

EU firefighters are already on the ground battling the flames side by side, thanks to the EU Civil Protection Mechanism. In Greece, Bulgarian fire fighters have joined Greek teams in Halkidiki. As we speak, Romanian firefighters are standing shoulder to shoulder with their French colleagues to fight the wildfires close to Narbonne and Marseilles. This is Europe in action. This is solidarity in action.

At the same time, we are bringing together Europe's top wildfire experts at the ERCC to help forecast risks, support planning and monitor the situation in real time. Of course, the best way to fight wildfires and to manage prolonged drought is to stay ahead of them, to anticipate and to prevent them from spiralling out of control. We also need to speed up our efforts to address the root causes of wildfires, climate change of course, drought, forest degradation and biodiversity loss. All of this makes wildfires more frequent, more intense, and also harder to stop.

Prevention is already a core part of our Common Agricultural Policy. And next year we will present an ambitious EU climate adaptation Plan. As highlighted in the recent report from the European Court of Auditors, more and more Member States are turning to EU funds to invest in prevention, and that's a step in the right direction. Between 2021 and 2027, EUR 18.8 billion are planned for investment in climate adaptation and risk management under cohesion policy. This includes EUR 3.3 billion dedicated to forestry alone.

Honourable Members this is exactly the kind of proactive, comprehensive and joined up approach that the Preparedness Union Strategy calls for. It is how we build resilience before disaster strikes. But let us be clear, preparedness needs resources. We need bold, sustained investment in prevention and preparedness. And of course, I do not forget recovery. Recovery is essential not just to rebuild, but to give hope and support to those affected by disaster. The EU Solidarity Fund is a vital tool to make sure that when crisis strikes, no country is left to face it alone.

That is why I count on your support to uphold a strong level of ambition in the upcoming MFF negotiations. This is not about political bargaining. It is about solidarity. The very soul of our European Union.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Lena DÃ¼pont, on behalf of the PPE Group. â Madam President, Madam Commissioner, Madam Minister, the Union Civil Protection Mechanism is a European success story. Since its creation, numerous emergency responses have been carried out. Equipment for firefighters and first responders have been financed and delivered. Firefighters are pre-positioned throughout Europe to help faster than before.

This is â like you just said, Madam Commissioner â European solidarity on the ground. As we speak, 22Â firefighting planes and four helicopters stand ready to support in the current wildfire season; 650Â firefighters from 14Â countries are pre-positioned in high-risk areas; more ground firefighting teams are ready to be mobilised.

And that solidarity is needed. If you compare this year's wildfire season to the previous, threats and dangers are multiplying. Colleagues from Portugal, from Spain, from Greece, from France will soon tell us about the devastating effects on the ground.

As the threats are emerging â man-made and natural â we also need to adapt as well. We need to be better prepared for larger incidents, for simultaneous and escalating incidents. And that means we need to scale up the pool of experts and the deployments; the investments in equipment and material, in resilience and readiness; the common risk assessment, the forecast and the situational picture. And, Madam Commissioner, we need to scale down on administrative hurdles â a common task for all of us here.

In the end, it is our task to support those on the ground who are bravely fighting wildfires today and floods tomorrow. We owe it to them. May they all return safe and sound from their deployments!

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Antonio Decaro, a nome del gruppo S&D. â Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, prendo la parola su questo tema a un anno dal mio primo intervento in plenaria sulla siccitÃ  e ad oggi, purtroppo, poco o niente Ã¨ cambiato.

Anzi, se proprio dobbiamo dirla tutta, la situazione Ã¨ peggiorata e il Sud e il centro dell'Europa vivono un'escalation delle temperature: 46Â gradi in Spagna e in Portogallo, oltre 40Â gradi in Italia e in Francia e l'indice dello zero termico sulle Alpi Ã¨ arrivato a superare i 5Â 000 metri; il Mar Mediterraneo ha registrato a giugno di quest'anno 1,9Â gradi in piÃ¹ rispetto alla media dei valori dei picchi registrati nel periodo tra il 2015 e il 2024, e nel 2025 gli incendi boschivi in Europa hanno giÃ  causato la distruzione di 196Â 000 ettari, 78Â 000 ettari in piÃ¹ rispetto all'anno precedente.

Questi numeri hanno un denominatore comune: la parola "emergenza".

Sapendo che siamo giÃ  in ritardo sulla programmazione strutturale, dobbiamo contrastare il cambiamento climatico e intervenire subito per preparare il territorio a questi fenomeni, investendo risorse adeguate e piani di gestione delle crisi efficienti, a partire dal prossimo bilancio europeo.

L'Europa non ha alternative, se vuole salvarsi. Prima ne prenderemo coscienza e prima potremo agire.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Sergio Berlato, a nome del gruppo ECR. â Signora Presidente, signora Commissaria, onorevoli colleghi, le istituzioni comunitarie e nazionali sono portate a puntare il dito accusatorio sui cambiamenti climatici per giustificare il costante verificarsi degli incendi boschivi.

La veritÃ  Ã¨ che il 96Â % degli incendi boschivi Ã¨ riconducibile alle attivitÃ  umane, sia accidentali sia dolose.

Questo ci insegna che non ha senso limitarsi a concentrare le risorse pubbliche sulle misure legate ai cambiamenti climatici, ma che Ã¨ piÃ¹ opportuno investire nella prevenzione legata al comportamento dei cittadini. La stragrande maggioranza degli incendi boschivi avviene o perchÃ© si lascia cadere sbadatamente un mozzicone di sigaretta o perchÃ© non si spegne bene un falÃ², oppure perchÃ© qualche criminale piromane appicca dolosamente il fuoco.

Cosa fare, quindi, per prevenire questi fenomeni o per intervenire prontamente quando queste situazioni avvengono? Bisogna prevenire, investendo nell'informazione e nell'educazione dei cittadini, rendendoli consapevoli che le conseguenze delle loro azioni possono essere pericolose per l'incolumitÃ  delle altre persone, costosissime dal punto di vista del danno economico e devastanti dal punto di vista ambientale e della perdita della biodiversitÃ .

Ma se, nonostante le azioni di prevenzione, dovessero comunque accadere alcuni di questi eventi, bisogna essere pronti per intervenire immediatamente per mettere in sicurezza le persone e ridurre al minimo il danno ambientale.

Approfitto dell'occasione per ringraziare tutte le donne e tutti gli uomini dei Vigili del fuoco e della Protezione Civile, che in modo encomiabile mettono a disposizione la loro professionalitÃ  e il loro coraggio, spesso rischiando la loro vita per garantire un servizio indispensabile alla collettivitÃ . A loro vada il nostro ringraziamento piÃ¹ sincero.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â GrÃ©gory Allione, au nom du groupe Renew. â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, Madame la Commissaire, Madame la Ministre, lâEurope suffoque, lâEurope sâembrase, les tempÃ©ratures dÃ©passent les 40Â Â°C dans tout le sud de lâEurope, des feux se sont dÃ©clarÃ©s aux Pays-Bas au mois de mars, et dâautres feux ravagent nos pays en ce moment mÃªme. Jâai une pensÃ©e pour mes amis engagÃ©s sur tous les thÃ©Ã¢tres opÃ©rationnels et, forcÃ©ment, pour toutes celles et tous ceux qui ont perdu un bien âÂ et encore, il nây a heureusement pas de victime Ã  ce stade.

En tant que sapeur-pompier depuis plus de trente ans, jâai eu Ã  lutter contre de nombreux feux. Aujourdâhui, jâai Ã  lutter et Ã  mener un combat ici mÃªme contre le dÃ©ni des climatosceptiques. Câest un combat difficile, et nous devons lutter tous ensemble contre cela. Pourtant, la science est sans appel, et le constat frappe Ã  nos portes tous les jours: dorÃ©navant, il faut anticiper et se prÃ©parer. Anticiper et se prÃ©parer; câest lÃ  tout le sens de la stratÃ©gie de notre Union europÃ©enne.

Pourtant, comment se prÃ©parer sans moyens financiers? Il va bien entendu falloir renforcer ce mÃ©canisme de protection civile, qui a Ã©tÃ© sollicitÃ© plus de 58Â fois lâan dernier. Il va falloir se prÃ©parer, car se prÃ©parer coÃ»tera toujours moins cher que rÃ©parer. Comment se prÃ©parer Ã©galement sans moyens suffisants? Nous avons les moyens, nous avons les compÃ©tences sur notre sol, et, au-delÃ  de lâurgence opÃ©rationnelle, il en va de notre souverainetÃ© et de notre autonomie stratÃ©gique.

Se prÃ©parer, câest aussi souligner et protÃ©ger lâengagement citoyen. Vous connaissez notre attachement sur le terrain, et plusieurs parlementaires y sont attachÃ©s.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Benedetta Scuderi, a nome del gruppo Verts/ALE. â Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, mentre spendiamo miliardi in armi, perdiamo contro il collasso climatico: temperature fino a 50Â gradi, siccitÃ , alluvioni, incendi.

Nessuna emergenza, quelle finiscono: Ã¨ un problema strutturale. Dobbiamo essere preparati con investimenti in prevenzione e infrastrutture, cittÃ  piÃ¹ verdi, leggi che difendano i lavoratori, energie rinnovabili e uscita dai fossili.

Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Valentina Palmisano, a nome del gruppo The Left. â Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, le crisi climatiche rendono le stagioni degli incendi sempre piÃ¹ lunghe, intense e devastanti e noi dobbiamo essere pronti.

Â Â RaÃºl de la Hoz Quintano (PPE). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, seÃ±ora comisaria, la temporada de incendios desgraciadamente ha comenzado ya. Lo saben muy bien en la provincia de Tarragona y en la regiÃ³n de Marsella, que no estÃ¡n viviendo una amenaza abstracta en forma de incendios, sino que estÃ¡n sufriendo en sus propias carnes una tragedia en tÃ©rminos humanos, en tÃ©rminos econÃ³micos y, por supuesto, tambiÃ©n en tÃ©rminos ecolÃ³gicos; es una amenaza, ademÃ¡s, que crece aÃ±o a aÃ±o.

Es verdad que la UniÃ³n Europea ha incrementado la financiaciÃ³n de medidas de lucha contra los incendios, pero es verdad tambiÃ©n ây hay que reconocerloâ que su eficacia es, hasta el momento, muy limitada. Falta una planificaciÃ³n a largo plazo, faltan criterios objetivos de reparto y falta centrarnos tambiÃ©n en la prevenciÃ³n y no tanto en la extinciÃ³n.

CoordinaciÃ³n para hacer mÃ¡s efectivas las medidas âmuchas veces financiadas por la propia UniÃ³n Europeaâ y tambiÃ©n para lograr una estrategia de planificaciÃ³n de la prevenciÃ³n a largo plazo y, junto a ello, por supuesto, inversiÃ³n real para mejorar las capacidades locales en la lucha contra los incendios y algo que para nosotros es extraordinariamente importante: la gestiÃ³n activa del territorio.

Necesitamos âpara que las polÃ­ticas de lucha contra incendios sean eficacesâ que nos centremos tambiÃ©n en la dinamizaciÃ³n de las Ã¡reas rurales y, por supuesto, en la lucha contra la despoblaciÃ³n.

Â Â Marta Temido (S&D). â Senhora Presidente, Senhora ComissÃ¡ria, Senhora Ministra, na UniÃ£o Europeia, este ano, as temperaturas, as Ã¡reas ardidas e o nÃºmero de incÃªndios jÃ¡ sÃ£o superiores Ã  mÃ©dia.

Â Â Diego Solier (ECR). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, seÃ±ora comisaria, los incendios no empiezan con el fuego, empiezan mucho antes: cuando se abandona el campo, cuando desaparecen los pastores, cuando las cabras ya no suben al monte, cuando los caminos se tapan, los cortafuegos se olvidan y los agricultores tiran la toalla.

Europa y los Gobiernos hablan mucho de medio ambiente, pero han expulsado a quienes lo cuidaban, han llenado el campo de papeles, de normativas que nadie entiende y de sanciones absurdas. Se olvidan de que donde hay un rebaÃ±o no hay maleza; donde hay un tractor hay vida.

Hoy nuestros ganaderos y agricultores son los Ãºnicos que hacen de bomberos sin uniforme y lo hacen gratis. Pero, mientras tanto, los fondos europeos no llegan, las ayudas no compensan y la polÃ­tica agrÃ­cola comÃºn castiga mÃ¡s de lo que apoya.

No se apaga un incendio con discursos: se apaga con pastores, con manos, con personas que aman la tierra. Si no protegemos a quienes cuidan el monte, el monte no nos protegerÃ¡ a nosotros y, entonces, volverÃ¡ a arder.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Gerben-Jan Gerbrandy (Renew). â Madam President, Madam Commissioner, obviously full support for your policy of preparedness Union. And it's obvious, like other colleagues have said, that we have to work closely together on these droughts and forest fires. And if you imagine that, even in April, in a country like the Netherlands, we already had forest fires â it says something.

But let's face it, the best way to deal with these crises is to prevent them. It's climate change that causes droughts, forest fires and floods, and it's threatening our livelihoods, our food security and even our lives. So, colleagues, let's invest in climate mitigation, in the 90Â % emission reduction in 2040 and in a sustainable agricultural system.

Today we voted and we made EURÂ 280Â million available to assist several Member States with the effects of the floods of the last couple of months. I hope that those parties who are voting against any climate change action have very, very deep pockets, because in a few years' time, we won't need EURÂ 280Â million, we will need billions of euros; in 10-15 years' time, we will need tens of billions of euros. And I count on you to pay that check.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Vicent MarzÃ  IbÃ¡Ã±ez (Verts/ALE). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, seÃ±ora comisaria, en mi paÃ­s este verano estamos sufriendo dos hechos histÃ³ricos. El primero, que ha sido el mes de junio mÃ¡s cÃ¡lido de la historia, con cuatro grados por encima de lo que es habitual en estas fechas. Y, al mismo tiempo, tambiÃ©n estamos sufriendo hoy mismo parques de bomberos cerrados âpor primera vez en la historia en veranoâ y ningÃºn refuerzo forestal para la temporada de verano.

Estas son las consecuencias diabÃ³licas de dos cuestiones. La primera, el cambio climÃ¡tico âclaro, evidenteâ, pero tambiÃ©n por tener negacionistas en los gobiernos, en nuestros gobiernos. Y eso estÃ¡ pasando en mi paÃ­s, pero pasarÃ¡ y estÃ¡ pasando tambiÃ©n en otros paÃ­ses de Europa. Por eso, necesitamos medidas valientes que de verdad hagan que se puedan aumentar los fondos para emergencias causadas por el cambio climÃ¡tico y que se pueda coordinar mejor el mecanismo de rescate europeo. Pero tambiÃ©n la ComisiÃ³n tiene que dar pasos valientes y dejar de pactar con aquellos que niegan el cambio climÃ¡tico.

Hace un aÃ±o usted y yo hablamos por telÃ©fono, casi un aÃ±o ây se lo agradezcoâ, con motivo de las inundaciones que se llevaron a 228 muertos en mi paÃ­s, y en los servicios de emergencias ahora nos dicen que estÃ¡n peor preparados que en ese momento y eso es inadmisible. Por eso, necesitamos mÃ¡s recursos y necesitamos mejor coordinaciÃ³n de los servicios de emergencias.

Â Â Matej Tonin (PPE). â Gospa predsednica! Komisarka! Kolegi!

Prihajam iz Slovenije, ki je bila v preteklosti Å¾rtev silovitih in obseÅ¾nih poÅ¾arov v naravi. V Sloveniji veÄ kot polovica obmoÄja predstavljajo gozdovi, zato so poÅ¾ari na nedostopnih obmoÄjih praktiÄno stalnica, stalna groÅ¾nja.

Sem tudi prostovoljni gasilec. Slovenija, ki ima dva milijona prebivalcev, ima 165.000 prostovoljnih gasilcev, od tega 50.000 operativnih gasilcev.

To govorim zato, ker Å¾elim poudariti, da za uÄinkovito gaÅ¡enje potrebujemo zadostno Å¡tevilo gasilcev in tudi ustrezno tehniko. In prav gasilci imajo kljuÄno vlogo, zlasti prostovoljni gasilci, so kljuÄni za odpornost na naravne in ÄloveÅ¡ko pogojene naravne nesreÄe.

So prvi, ki se odzovejo. Oni so tisti, ki poznajo obmoÄje. Trdno sem prepriÄan, da prava unija pripravljenosti ne more obstajati brez jasnega priznanja tega ÄloveÅ¡kega kapitala.

Evropska unija bi morala tudi finanÄno spodbujati prostovoljne gasilce, predvsem pa takoj odpraviti vse birokratizme, ki onemogoÄajo prostovoljstvo.

Ljudje, ki se dajo na razpolago, potrebujejo tudi ustrezno tehniko. Slovenija je v zadnjih letih okrepila svojo zraÄno komponento pri gaÅ¡enju poÅ¾arov. Prav zraÄne sile so tiste, ki lahko bistveno prispevajo k pogasitvi poÅ¾arov na nedostopnih obmoÄjih.

Od Evropske unije priÄakujem, da bo poveÄala nabor razpoloÅ¾ljive tehnike, ki bi jo Älanice lahko delile.

Â Â

Â Â Leire PajÃ­n (S&D). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, en los Ãºltimos dÃ­as una ola de calor ha barrido Europa de norte a sur. La sequÃ­a amenaza a todas las regiones, no solo al sur y no solo al MediterrÃ¡neo, aunque en el MediterrÃ¡neo sea devastador. Hay incendios en Grecia, en Alemania, en Francia, en Serbia y en Escocia. Se han perdido dos vidas humanas en Lleida y luchamos ante las consecuencias en Tarragona.

Los fenÃ³menos meteorolÃ³gicos extremos por el cambio climÃ¡tico ponen en riesgo la salud, la economÃ­a y nuestras vidas. Si algo hemos aprendido de la negligente y dolorosa gestiÃ³n de la dana en la Comunidad Valenciana ây tambiÃ©n de lo que estÃ¡ ocurriendo en Texasâ es la importancia de llegar a tiempo, de alertar a la poblaciÃ³n y tambiÃ©n de la inversiÃ³n y la necesidad de tener buenos recursos pÃºblicos y recursos humanos, de conservar la naturaleza y de luchar contra el cambio climÃ¡tico.

SeÃ±ora comisaria, tenemos que blindar la estrategia europea con recursos y con instrumentos; la vamos a apoyar para eso. Necesitamos implicar a los Gobiernos, a las instituciones, a la ciudadanÃ­a y tambiÃ©n a la ciencia. Tenemos que mejorar los sistemas y garantizar la coordinaciÃ³n entre ellos. Estos dÃ­as tendremos la oportunidad, con el marco financiero, de saber si estamos a la altura y tambiÃ©n de ver si la presidenta se alÃ­a con los desinformadores y los negacionistas o con los que queremos llegar a tiempo y salvar vidas.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Julien Leonardelli (PfE). â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, comme chaque Ã©tÃ©, lâEurope du Sud et le sud de la France sâembrasent. Et comme chaque Ã©tÃ©, lâÃtat franÃ§ais, pourtant obnubilÃ© par la question du rÃ©chauffement climatique, est dÃ©passÃ©. Chez moi, en Occitanie, en une semaine, 4Â 000Â hectares sont partis en fumÃ©e dans lâAude, malgrÃ© le courage hÃ©roÃ¯que de nos pompiers. Ã Marseille, câest la mÃªme tragÃ©die. Et que fait le gouvernement franÃ§ais? Il constate, il promet, mais il nâagit pas.

Nos moyens aÃ©riens, autrefois puissants, sont de nos jours dramatiquement insuffisants. Lâan dernier, nous nâavions que deux Canadair disponibles en pleine saison, faute dâentretien. Dâailleurs, notre flotte est vieillissante et la promesse dâEmmanuelÂ Macron de la renouveler est dÃ©jÃ  enterrÃ©e, avec seulement deux avions supplÃ©mentaires âÂ au mieuxÂ â dâici Ã Â 2028. Pis encore, la France a refusÃ© un financement europÃ©en pour des hÃ©licoptÃ¨res bombardiers dâeau, quâelle loue dÃ©sormais Ã  prix fort.

Pendant ce temps, le voyage en absurdie continue. On subventionne lâarrachage des vignes, qui sont de vÃ©ritables pare-feu naturels, pour laisser place Ã  des friches inflammables. On bloque des amÃ©nagements hydrauliques au nom dâun Ã©cologisme dogmatique alors que les territoires brÃ»lent. Alors non, le feu nâest pas une fatalitÃ©. Câest une faute, une faute dâÃtat.

(L'orateur accepte plusieurs questions carton bleu)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â GrÃ©gory Allione (Renew), question Â«carton bleuÂ». â Monsieur Leonardelli, bien entendu, on se connaÃ®t et, forcÃ©ment, on a des sujets en commun. Vous m'avez entendu parler, j'espÃ¨re, et aujourd'hui, mon combat, ce n'est plus les feux de forÃªt âÂ mÃªme si cet Ã©tÃ©, je prends encore des gardes. Nous avons eu le mÃªme type de rÃ©action ce matin, Ã  propos de nos objectifs pour dÃ©carboner notre Europe. Cela fait plus de 36Â ans que je suis sapeur-pompier. J'ai vu l'intensitÃ© et la virulence des feux contre lesquels j'ai luttÃ©, j'ai vu les inondations dans cette Occitanie qui vous est chÃ¨re.

Ces combats, je les mÃ¨ne depuis quelques annÃ©es et je vois l'Ã©volution du dÃ©rÃ¨glement climatique. Je vous en prie, puisque vous tenez des propos pour soutenir l'action europÃ©enne en faveur des sapeurs-pompiers et des forces de protection civile, agissez aussi en votant pour dÃ©carboner, pour faire en sorte que notre climat soit plus agrÃ©able et, en tout cas, qu'il n'y ait pas de consÃ©quences sur nos forces de protection civile et surtout sur nos concitoyens et les habitations qui leur sont chÃ¨res.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Julien Leonardelli (PfE), rÃ©ponse carton bleu. â Monsieur Allione, effectivement, je connais votre combat et votre soutien aux pompiers. Vous-mÃªme, vous avez Ã©tÃ© pompier dans votre carriÃ¨re, et ce combat est fort et remarquable. Mais, vous le savez aussi bien que moi: vous Ãªtes ici, dans cet hÃ©micycle, le reprÃ©sentant d'EmmanuelÂ Macron. Vous savez aussi bien que moi que le parc est vieillissant et qu'il y a trente ans de retard en France. Aujourd'hui, les pompiers sont en premiÃ¨re ligne et ils ont besoin de moyens, ils ont besoin d'un investissement fort âÂ chose que vous n'avez pas su faire depuis maintenant de nombreuses annÃ©es. Donc, quand nous enÂ arriverons Ã  devoir, nous, prendre ce type de responsabilitÃ©s, croyez bien qu'avec Marine LeÂ Pen nous donnerons des moyens aux pompiers.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Thomas Pellerin-Carlin (S&D), question Â«carton bleuÂ». â Monsieur Leonardelli, nous, dÃ©putÃ©s europÃ©ens, sommes reprÃ©sentants des citoyens qui nous ont Ã©lus. Ainsi, quand vous vous adressez Ã  des gens qui ont servi la patrie en Ã©tant pompiers, comme notre collÃ¨gue GrÃ©goryÂ Allione, vous vous adressez Ã  lui, et pas Ã  une personne qui reprÃ©senterait le chef dâun Ãtat âÂ premiÃ¨rement.

DeuxiÃ¨mement, nous savons quâil y a âÂ excusez-moi, je vois que vous dodelinez de la tÃªte, câest comme Ã§a que Ã§a fonctionne en dÃ©mocratie, mÃªme si je sais que câest un mot qui est peu pratiquÃ© dans votre parti politiqueâ¦ NÃ©anmoins, ma question est simple: quâavez-vous votÃ© ce midi lorsque nous votions sur la procÃ©dure dâurgence pour agir sur le climat? Rappelez-moi quel a Ã©tÃ© votre vote.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Julien Leonardelli (PfE), rÃ©ponse carton bleu. â Monsieur Pellerin-Carlin, nous ne rentrerons jamais dans vos dÃ©lires Ã©cologistes, qui tuent nos territoires. Les premiers Ã©cologistes sont les agriculteurs, et nous, contrairement Ã  vous, nous les dÃ©fendons, et, face aux feux de forÃªt, les viticulteurs sont les premiers dÃ©fenseurs.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Presidente. â Abbiamo altre blue card, ma procediamo con il dibattito. Prego, onorevole Razza.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Ruggero Razza (ECR). â Signora Presidente, signora Commissaria, onorevoli colleghi, da quando Ã¨ iniziata questa legislatura non Ã¨ il primo dibattito in cui ci occupiamo di incendi, non Ã¨ il primo dibattito in cui ci occupiamo di siccitÃ .

A me, come parlamentare italiano, Ã¨ capitato di rivolgere molti mesi fa un'interrogazione alla Commissione europea nella quale ho chiesto conto delle scelte che sono state fatte, per esempio per l'acquisto dei Canadair.

Abbiamo la sensazione che si sia in ritardo sulla tabella di marcia, ma soprattutto abbiamo la sensazione che si sia scelto una tecnologia â probabilmente ancora oggi l'unica presente â che perÃ² Ã¨ fortemente obsoleta, che non Ã¨ adeguata nel numero alle esigenze di pericolo crescenti, che non mette al cuore la digitalizzazione per creare delle forme di prevenzione che possano salvaguardare i nostri territori.

Si Ã¨ detto nel dibattito che la gran parte degli incendi Ã¨ legata a matrice dolosa, ma l'espansione di questi incendi nella maggior parte dei casi Ã¨ dovuta al fatto che si riesce a scoprirli tardi e che non si riesce ad agire presto.

Ci si attende da parte della Commissione, proprio nella strategia della preparazione, che vi sia attenzione al partenariato europeo per andare oltre una tecnologia adeguata e obsoleta.

(L'oratore accetta di rispondere a una domanda "cartellino blu")

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Gerben-Jan Gerbrandy (Renew), blue-card question. â Madam President, MrÂ Razza, I listened carefully to you, and I think we all agree that we have to fight forest fires. But something else is that we have the responsibility to avoid a further increase in forest fires, and one of the most important reasons for this increase is climate change. Whether you like it or not, it is there and it's causing the enormous increase in forest fires.

What are you doing? You're a powerful man, because you're a Member of the European Parliament. You decide on climate policy. What do you do to help avoid further forest fires in Europe and the rest of the world?

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Ruggero Razza (ECR), risposta a una domanda "cartellino blu". â Non penso che il Parlamento europeo, collega, abbia la possibilitÃ  di evitare ulteriori incendi.Â Certamente, le politiche europee e le politiche nazionali possono prevedere delle strategie di adattamento al clima, ma prevedere delle strategie di adattamento al clima â io sono italiano, lo ha fatto anche il governo italiano lavorando al piano di adattamento al clima â significa lavorare sulla prevenzione, significa lavorare su ciÃ² che riguarda la prevenzione idrogeologica e significa determinare tutte quelle azioni che servono ad accompagnare il tempo in cui viviamo verso la possibilitÃ  che non ci siano emergenze.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Presidente. â Grazie onorevole. Ci sono altre, ma procediamo. Prego, onorevole Vasile, che non vedo. Prego, onorevole StaÄ·is.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â MÄrtiÅÅ¡ StaÄ·is (Verts/ALE). â MadamÂ President, colleagues, crises are part of Europe. But when disaster strikes, it's not a governmental official who knocks first at your door â it's your neighbour. And that is where true preparedness begins: in trust between people; in the strength of local NGOs and community groups, who know the names and needs in their neighbourhoods. They can organise training, build resilience from the bottom up, and often they are more trusted than government institutions.

We see this in Finland, where networks like Vapepa link citizens and state, and across Europe, like in Latvia, where the Latvian Platform for Development Cooperation works to build local resilience.

We must support the human infrastructure with recognition, with resources and with a seat at the table, because resilience starts with the person next door, not the system above. So let's include strong support for NGOs in the EU preparedness strategy as well.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Â Â Ana Miguel Pedro (PPE). â Senhora Presidente, se hÃ¡ risco que no meu paÃ­s, Portugal, conhecemos demasiado bem Ã© o dos incÃªndios florestais. SÃ³ em 2024 arderam 137 000 hectares, perderamâse 16 vidas. A Ã¡rea ardida foi quatro vezes superior Ã  de 2023 e 2025 jÃ¡ nos deu o mÃªs de junho mais quente de sempre.

Ã tempo de romper com a lÃ³gica reativa. A Europa precisa de uma nova cultura de preparaÃ§Ã£o estrutural. Muito se fala hoje de defesa e bem. Mas se falamos de defesa, entÃ£o temos de falar de fogos florestais, porque hoje, em Portugal, em grande parte do sul da Europa, a defesa comeÃ§a na floresta, comeÃ§a na proteÃ§Ã£o do territÃ³rio, comeÃ§a na antecipaÃ§Ã£o do risco.

Em 2024, quase 5Â 000 militares portugueses estiveram empenhados no combate aos incÃªndios, patrulhas de vigilÃ¢ncia do exÃ©rcito, drones da ForÃ§a AÃ©rea. Este Ã© o novo teatro de operaÃ§Ãµes. NÃ£o Ã© um campo de batalha convencional, mas estÃ¡ em causa a mesma coisa: a seguranÃ§a das populaÃ§Ãµes, a integridade territorial do territÃ³rio nacional.

A verdadeira defesa comeÃ§a, na realidade, antes do incÃªndio. ComeÃ§a na floresta gerida, no campo cuidado, nas comunidades atentas, no territÃ³rio habitado. E, sim, precisamos de acelerar o rescEU, mais meios aÃ©reos, sobretudo para os paÃ­ses da linha da frente: Portugal, Espanha, GrÃ©cia. A prontidÃ£o europeia nÃ£o pode ser adiada quando o risco Ã© cÃ­clico e previsÃ­vel. Precisamos tambÃ©m de mobilizar os fundos de coesÃ£o, agrÃ­colas, virados para a prevenÃ§Ã£o, nÃ£o apenas para a reconstruÃ§Ã£o. E precisamos de uma campanha europeia de educaÃ§Ã£o para a preparaÃ§Ã£o da escola, o municÃ­pio.

A resiliÃªncia tem de entrar no quotidiano. ResiliÃªncia Ã© uma batalha e Ã© uma batalha que temos que vencer, antes das chamas, nÃ£o depois das cinzas.

(A oradora recusa uma pergunta Â«cartÃ£o azulÂ» de Ana Miranda Paz)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Thomas Pellerin-Carlin (S&D). â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, Marseille brÃ»le, et lâextrÃªme droite regarde ailleurs. Les flammes ont atteint le quartier de lâEstaque. Je pense aux marins-pompiers de Marseille et aux pompiers des SDIS qui continuent Ã  lutter contre les flammes. Je pense aussi aux Marseillais, Ã  mes amis âÂ Ãlisa, LÃ©a, Christophe, Pascaline.

Je ressens aussi une profonde colÃ¨re car cela fait trenteÂ ans que les scientifiques du GIEC alertent sur le fait que les dÃ©rÃ¨glements climatiques intensifient les incendies. Soyons clairs: notre soutien ne doit pas se limiter Ã  des mots. Ce nâest pas une vidÃ©o TikTok qui sauvera Marseille des flammes, mais le courage des pompiers. Ce sera aussi notre courage politique, que de sâattaquer aux racines du mal: lâurgence climatique.Â Or, aujourdâhui mÃªme, la majoritÃ© de droite et dâextrÃªme droite de ce Parlement europÃ©en a rejetÃ© notre demande dâadopter en urgence la proposition de loi europÃ©enne sur le climat.

Chers collÃ¨gues, rÃ©agissons avant quâil ne soit trop tard. Agissons avant dâentrer dans un monde oÃ¹ le courage des pompiers ne suffira plus pour sauver Marseille des flammes.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Ana Vasconcelos (Renew). â Senhora Presidente, Senhora ComissÃ¡ria, colegas, os portugueses conhecem bem o custo da impreparaÃ§Ã£o. Em 2022, arderam mais de 120Â 000 hectares, quase o dobro da mÃ©dia da Ãºltima dÃ©cada. Perdemos mais de 30 % da Ã¡gua tratada, bem acima de paÃ­ses como a Dinamarca e a Holanda, que perdem menos 10 % e nÃ£o enfrentam secas regulares como nÃ³s.

FenÃ³menos climÃ¡ticos cada vez mais extremos sÃ£o o novo normal. Portanto, quando secas, incÃªndios, inundaÃ§Ãµes regressam ano apÃ³s ano, nÃ£o sÃ£o fenÃ³menos inesperados, sÃ£o falhas de governaÃ§Ã£o.

Ã bom sinal ver a ComissÃ£o mobilizar meios aÃ©reos, investir em sistemas de alerta e desenvolver cenÃ¡rios de risco. Mas deve ficar claro que os Estados-Membros tÃªm de resolver problemas como o abandono de terras e o desperdÃ­cio de Ã¡gua na rede. HÃ¡ paÃ­ses, como Portugal, que nem conseguem concluir o cadastro da sua propriedade rural. Assim, Ã© impossÃ­vel haver gestÃ£o florestal, que deve ser a principal prioridade na prevenÃ§Ã£o de incÃªndios.

HÃ¡ uma lÃ³gica econÃ³mica evidente: remediar sai mais caro do que prevenir. E os fundos europeus devem premiar quem se prepara, nÃ£o sustentar ineficiÃªncias.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Ana Miranda Paz (Verts/ALE). â Senhora Presidente, hÃ¡ paÃ­ses que proÃ­bem indÃºstrias nocivas, como Portugal, que impÃµe uma moratÃ³ria Ã  plantaÃ§Ã£o de eucalipto.

HÃ¡ paÃ­ses que permitem destruir a natureza, o solo, a agricultura, com moratÃ³rias interessadas sobre o eucalipto, como Ã© o governo do Partido Popular no meu paÃ­s, a Galiza.

Querem terminar com os baldios (a oradora falou numa lÃ­ngua nÃ£o oficial da UE), em vez de combater a causa dos incÃªndios, para deixar o solo e a terra ao serviÃ§o da indÃºstria da celulose, como as empresas Ence e Altri, que querem comprar na Galiza o que nÃ£o as deixam fazer em Portugal.

O eucalipto facilita o aumento dos incÃªndios, favorece o aumento especulativo, promovido por governos incompetentes que favorecem as empresas da macrocelulose. O projeto de Altri exigiria plantar entre 90Â 000 e 160Â 000 hectares, alÃ©m dos 409Â 000 existentes para satisfazer as necessidades desta indÃºstria.

A Europa exige plantar mais Ã¡rvores para aumentar a massa florestal e o governo galego, pelo contrÃ¡rio, ataca a biodiversidade, nÃ£o favorece a plantaÃ§Ã£o de espÃ©cies autÃ³ctones e converte a Galiza no maior eucaliptal da Europa para dar mais peso Ã  indÃºstria de celulose. Â Â PÃ©ter Magyar (PPE). â Tisztelt ElnÃ¶k Asszony! Biztos Asszony! A gyÃ¶nyÃ¶rÅ± hazÃ¡mat az utÃ³bbi Ã©vekben immÃ¡r sokadszor sÃºjtja rendkÃ­vÃ¼li szÃ¡razsÃ¡g. Az aszÃ¡ly egy termÃ©szeti csapÃ¡s. Â Â Victor Negrescu (S&D). â DoamnÄ preÈedintÄ, doamnÄ comisar, dragi colegi, Uniunea EuropeanÄ trebuie sÄ treacÄ de la reacÈie la prevenÈie.

Incendiile Èi seceta nu mai sunt excepÈii, sunt noua normalitate. Ãn RomÃ¢nia, am vÄzut cum temperaturile extreme au distrus culturi Ã®n TimiÈ, Alba sau Olt, cum vÃ¢ntul puternic a aprins cÃ¢mpurile din Giurgiu Èi Teleorman. La ConstanÈa, fermierii s-au trezit cu pÄmÃ¢ntul secat, iar Ã®n multe sate pompierii au intervenit cu echipamente vechi sau insuficiente.

Ca negociator-Èef al bugetului UE pentru 2025, am obÈinut fonduri suplimentare pentru situaÈiile de urgenÈÄ, mai multe resurse pentru dotarea autoritÄÈilor locale, sprijin direct pentru comunitÄÈile vulnerabile Èi crearea unei capacitÄÈi europene reale de rÄspuns anticipativ.

Dar trebuie continuat. Nu putem lÄsa comunitÄÈile singure Ã®n faÈa crizelor. O uniune pregÄtitÄ este o Uniune care previne, nu doar intervine, iar prevenÈia trebuie sÄ devinÄ un pilon central Ã®n bugetul Èi politicile noastre europene.

Â Â Marjan Å arec (Renew). â Gospa predsednica, vÂ danaÅ¡njih Äasih je kljuÄno, da kot druÅ¾ba postanemo bolj odporni na vse morebitne groÅ¾nje naÅ¡emu prebivalstvu. Sem vsekakor spada tudi pripravljenost na naravne katastrofe.Â Strategija EU o pripravljenosti na krize govori tudi o tem, kar pomeni, da se komisarka dobro zaveda celotne problematike.

Kot nekdanji minister za obrambo, pristojen tudi za to podroÄje, se zavedam, da je kljuÄno sodelovanje gasilcev, civilne zaÅ¡Äite in vojske. V naÅ¡em primeru se je to pokazalo za odloÄilno. Nujno se je zavedati, da vojska ne pomeni samo oroÅ¾ja, temveÄ je pomen bistveno Å¡irÅ¡i. Zato je nujno vlagati tudi v dvojno rabo oziroma sisteme, ki v kljuÄnem trenutku pomenijo razliko med Å¾ivljenjem in smrtjo.

A brez prave preventive ni in ne bo uspeha. RazmiÅ¡ljati moramo Å¡irÅ¡e: kako bomo kot druÅ¾ba in gospodarstvo bolj odporni in trajnostni.

SpoÅ¡tovanje narave naj postane naÅ¡a prva obrambna linija.

Fattar ni inte varfÃ¶r, klimatfÃ¶rnekare? Blundar ni fÃ¶r att TBE-fallen har fÃ¶rdubblats i mitt land pÃ¥ bara tio Ã¥r? Avesta. BorlÃ¤nge. Smedjebacken. SÃ¤ter. Sandviken. Hudik. SÃ¶derhamn. Â Â Giusi Princi (PPE). â Signora Presidente, signora Commissaria, onorevoli colleghi, quello degli incendi e della siccitÃ  Ã¨ ormai un appuntamento drammatico con cui l'Europa deve fare i conti ogni estate.

Vengo da una regione, la Calabria, che con la sua alta densitÃ  boschiva â 650Â 000 ettari di patrimonio verde â conosce bene questa sfida. E mi piace segnalare come proprio la Calabria sia diventata un modello positivo per l'Italia, che potrebbe benissimo essere assunto a modello europeo.

L'operazione Tolleranza zero, voluta dal presidente di Regione, Roberto Occhiuto, ha portato infatti risultati straordinari: -77Â % di superficie bruciata grazie a 28 droni per monitoraggio continuo, coordinamento tecnologico e azione preventiva, una strategia che ha ridotto del 57Â % le richieste di intervento aereo alla flotta nazionale.

La Calabria dimostra pertanto che se l'Europa fa prevenzione spende di meno e salvaguarda il suo prezioso patrimonio boschivo.

U 2023. godini Europa je izgubila viÅ¡e od 500 tisuÄa hektara Å¡uma, a pred nama je joÅ¡ jedna izuzetno topla sezona.

RescEUÂ flota, koja je proÅ¡le godine imala 28 aviona i Äetiri helikoptera, igrat Äe kljuÄnu ulogu u brzoj pomoÄi drÅ¾avama Älanicama. Dolazim iz Hrvatske koja je, naÅ¾alost, sve viÅ¡e suoÄena sa Å¡umskim poÅ¾arima i snaÅ¾no podupirem ove napore. ProÅ¡le godine potpisali smo ugovor o nabavci dva nova kanadera, upravo iz ovog mehanizma, i trebali bi biti isporuÄeni 2027./2028.

Pokazali smo i veliku operativnu spremnost u razliÄitim ugrozama i mislim da moÅ¾emo podijeliti ta iskustva s razliÄitim zemljama Älanicama. UloÅ¾ili smo i 634 milijunaÂ EURÂ u sustav protupoÅ¾arne zaÅ¡tite i dodatnih 55 milijunaÂ EURÂ u nabavku nove opreme.

MeÄutim, Unija pripravnosti mora u svojim planovima biti fleksibilnija, mora imati ciljanu i dostupnu podrÅ¡ku prilagoÄenu lokalnim potrebama. ZakljuÄno, potrebni su nam prevencija i operativni planovi, racionalna ulaganja u sigurnost i odrÅ¾ivost.

Â Â Rosa Serrano Sierra (S&D). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, el aÃ±o 2024 ha sido el mÃ¡s caluroso registrado en Europa. Hemos vivido episodios de sequÃ­as histÃ³ricas y calor extremo y tambiÃ©n, al mismo tiempo, hemos sufrido las mayores inundaciones desde 2013.

Esos desastres naturales ya no son una excepciÃ³n y tienen una causa clara: la emergencia climÃ¡tica, cuyas consecuencias âcomo hemos podido comprobarâ son cada vez mÃ¡s graves.

Reducir el impacto del cambio climÃ¡tico en Europa tiene que ser una prioridad y, para ello, nuestros territorios necesitan recursos. El prÃ³ximo presupuesto y el nuevo plan europeo de adaptaciÃ³n al cambio climÃ¡tico tendrÃ¡n que contar con una dotaciÃ³n presupuestaria suficiente y, en ese contexto, luchar contra los discursos negacionistas es una responsabilidad polÃ­tica, porque hoy sabemos que la prevenciÃ³n salva vidas y los socialistas estaremos siempre frente a las ausencias de aquellos que se niegan a asumir esta tarea. Estaremos siempre del lado de nuestros territorios, de las personas y del tejido productivo.

Â Â SÃ©rgio Humberto (PPE). â Senhora Presidente, Senhora ComissÃ¡ria, caros colegas, o meu ensinamento enquanto presidente da CÃ¢mara Municipal em Portugal, ensinouâme muitas coisas, uma das quais: Â«Ã melhor prevenir do que remediar. Better safe than sorry.Â»

A melhor resposta ao risco crescente de incÃªndios e secas assenta na sensatez de nos prevenirmos. Foi com um impulso do PPE no Parlamento Europeu que a UniÃ£o reforÃ§ou o programa rescEU com mais aviÃµes, mais equipas de intervenÃ§Ã£o e mais fundos para a gestÃ£o sustentÃ¡vel da floresta. SÃ³ este ano foram mobilizados mais de 500 milhÃµes de EUR para a prevenÃ§Ã£o, e resposta, de desastres naturais.

O caminho Ã© este. Uma verdadeira UniÃ£o Europeia Ã© uma UniÃ£o preparada, uma UniÃ£o resiliente e cooperante entre si, disponÃ­vel a partilhar o esforÃ§o coletivo por um amanhÃ£ melhor.

Â Â Francisco JosÃ© MillÃ¡n Mon (PPE). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, los incendios forestales exigen una respuesta coordinada a nivel europeo. La llamada UniÃ³n de PreparaciÃ³n me parece una meta importante. Por supuesto, debemos anticiparnos, pero la prevenciÃ³n no es suficiente: tambiÃ©n debemos ser capaces de reaccionar con eficacia en la extinciÃ³n.

En EspaÃ±a, las comunidades autÃ³nomas competentes para la extinciÃ³n de incendios sufragan solas los gastos. Los fondos FEDER deberÃ­an ser usados tambiÃ©n para la extinciÃ³n, no solo para la prevenciÃ³n. En este sentido, celebro el impulso al instrumento rescEU âque nos ha explicado la comisariaâ, que dotarÃ¡ a la UniÃ³n Europea de una flota permanente cada vez mÃ¡s voluminosa de extinciÃ³n de incendios, de aviones y de helicÃ³pteros.

Voy a hablar de Galicia, que es mi tierra. No me puedo detener en las falsedades que acaba de esparcir una diputada gallega que quiere hacer olvidar el fracaso del Gobierno bipartito hace unos veinte aÃ±os en la extinciÃ³n de incendios y que contrasta con el buen hacer de la Xunta de Galicia del PP. Lo que quiero destacar es que tenemos en Galicia el Centro Integral de Lucha contra el Fuego, situado en Ourense, exactamente en ToÃ©n, que se estÃ¡ consolidando como un autÃ©ntico campus internacional de referencia para la formaciÃ³n conjunta. Este aÃ±o recibiremos allÃ­ a bomberos forestales de Estonia y tambiÃ©n de los PaÃ­ses Bajos.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Vytenis Povilas Andriukaitis (S&D). â Madam President, dear colleagues, dear Commissioner, of course, wildfire has no longer affected only southern Europe, but also erupted in northern and central regions of the European Union and wildfires are now no longer a seasonal phenomenon. They are a persistent, systemic threat and they require a systemic response.

Over 150 experts from the EU proposed an integrated wildfire risk management strategy. One of the strategy's central ambitions is to help shift the EU from a fragmented, reactive response toward a shared, proactive framework to support long-term resilience and civil protection capacity. In a context where climate change is reshaping geography and the intensity of fire risk, this shift is both urgent and complex. I think we need to include it into our preparedness Union plans.

Â

Varautuneen maan palkaksi on siis jÃ¤Ã¤nyt huolehtia myÃ¶s niistÃ¤, jotka eivÃ¤t varautumista ole yhtÃ¤ vakavissaan ottaneet. Â Â Ciaran Mullooly (Renew). â MadamÂ President, CommissionerÂ Lahbib, the people of Ireland will not forget what you did for them when StormÂ Ãowyn hit our shores last January. Your emergency response mechanism sent 18 industrial generators to Ireland. The lights came back; the water came back. Our government will hopefully learn to trigger that mechanism earlier.

Fortunately, we do not have widespread wildfires in Ireland, but the small coastal community of Achill in County Mayo certainly has an issue. In the last three years, they've had 12Â wildfires, three of them so far this year. And indeed, their village was evacuated last year.

They want to speak to you and to the Commission about a new policy, a strategy to deal with the situation â a national farm and land management strategy. They say their lives, their homes and their farming livelihoods and even the designated conservation areas are under threat.

So, they want joined-up thinking, Commissioner. They want the Commission and the Irish Government to work together with all the agencies. They do not want a 'pass the box' â somebody saying, 'It is not my job to put up the signs, to speak to the tourists. It is not my job to speak to the farmers.' We ask for a joined-up approach, support the people of Achill in County Mayo.

Â Â Diana Riba i Giner (Verts/ALE). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, (la oradora se expresa en una lengua no oficial), en el Ãºltimo mes, tres incendios en CataluÃ±a han arrasado mÃ¡s de quince mil hectÃ¡reas. En los Ãºltimos aÃ±os, CataluÃ±a ha sufrido la sequÃ­a mÃ¡s severa de la historia moderna, tanto en persistencia como en intensidad, y este mes de junio ha sido el mÃ¡s cÃ¡lido jamÃ¡s registrado. Y el mar MediterrÃ¡neo ha alcanzado temperaturas tropicales.

Pero Â¿saben cuÃ¡l es el peligro mÃ¡s grave? El Â«terraplanismoÂ» climÃ¡tico que domina la bancada de la derecha en este Pleno. Y me temo que, si seguimos dando la responsabilidad de redactar las leyes climÃ¡ticas a la extrema derecha, como estÃ¡ haciendo el PPE, esto no se resolverÃ¡.

No cedamos ante la ignorancia de la extrema derecha, ante su Â«cuÃ±adismoÂ» climÃ¡tico. Necesitamos una Europa que crea en la ciencia y que actÃºe en la evidencia, porque lo que estÃ¡ en juego es nuestra propia existencia.

Â Â Diana Iovanovici ÅoÅoacÄ (NI). â DoamnÄ preÈedintÄ, vÄ mulÈumesc cÄ aÈi menÈionat RomÃ¢nia Èi pompierii romÃ¢ni, pentru cÄ Ã®n fiecare an, pompierii romÃ¢ni ajutÄ Ã®n toate ÈÄrile, dar din pÄcate nu ÈtiÈi problemele pompierilor romÃ¢ni.

Sunt plÄtiÈi cu 500-600 de euro pe lunÄ Èi dupÄ ce cÄ sunt pompieri, mai mai fac Èi pe paramedicii la SMURD. SMURD-ul acela, pe care dumneavoastrÄ Ã®l ovaÈionaÈi, condus de Raed Arafat, care omoarÄ oameni. Pentru cÄ SMURD-ul nu Ã®Èi acordÄ nici un prim-ajutor. I-am auzit pe domnul Negrescu Èi pe domnul Buda vorbind, dar ei reprezintÄ socialiÈtii Èi liberalii, care conduc RomÃ¢nia de 35 de ani Èi spun cÄ pompierii nu au echipamente. PÄi din cauza lor nu au echipamente, cÄ furÄ banii. Acum doi ani de zile, la incendiul de la Crevedia, pompierii erau sÄ fie linÈaÈi de guvern Èi acuzaÈi pentru cÄ nu au apÄrat oamenii, deÈi i-au salvat, din cauza unor afaceri dubioase ale socialiÈtilor Èi liberalilor cu gaz lichefiat.

Ästa este adevÄrul despre problemele pompierilor, care sunt eroi ai RomÃ¢niei.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

(Fine della procedura "catch the eye")

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Hadja Lahbib, Member of the Commission. â Madam President, honourable Members, thank you very much for this vivid, fruitful debate, for sharing your thoughts, your experience and your insights. I will start by saying that the Commission's approach to preparing for the wildfire and drought season goes far beyond simply reacting to disasters. We are focused on building long-term resilience through prevention, risk management, preparedness and clear communication.

Honourable Member Lena DÃ¼pont, you are right when you say that we need to scale up our approach to preparedness â that's exactly what we are doing through preparedness by design. When we say 'preparedness by design', it's about â without introducing any administrative burden â not only strengthening the EU Civil Protection Mechanism that we will call UCPM 2.0, but also mainstreaming preparedness in many other instruments.

With the EU Preparedness Union strategy, we have, therefore, committed to embed preparedness into all relevant Commission policies and we are getting everyone â everyone â on board. We will step up our efforts to raise public awareness about wildfire risks and strengthen cooperation with the private sector.

When it comes to the question of the choice of the candidates for the purchase of 12Â rescue planes, the European Commission engaged in several discussions with potential manufacturers and ultimately, the decision â which was taken by the national authorities of the Member States â was based on technical, operational and economic factors. And at the time, the CL-515 from De Havilland Canada was the only specialised, medium, amphibious firefighting aircraft available on the market. Unfortunately, no comparable European alternatives existed then due to the relatively small market for this type of plane. And that said, we remain hopeful that European manufacturers will play a more significant role in this market in the future. We already discussed this issue with the Commissioner and Vice-President, StÃ©phane SÃ©journÃ©.

Honourable Member GrÃ©gory Allione:

Merci tout dâabord pour votre engagement, et je tiens, je vous assure, autant que vous Ã  lâengagement citoyen. Câest dâailleurs une des recommandations du rapport NiinistÃ¶, qui souligne que nous ne prenons pas suffisamment en compte lâincroyable force de solidaritÃ© qui sommeille en chaque citoyen. Les citoyens sont le premier pilier, la base fondatrice de notre stratÃ©gie, et il est vrai que sans eux rien nâest possible et que nous avons besoin, Ã©videmment, de volontaires, de mÃªme quâil nous faut encourager une nouvelle gÃ©nÃ©ration Ã  se former, Ã  Ãªtre prÃªte Ã  faire face aux dangers et Ã  agir. Nous devons aussi avoir le sens des responsabilitÃ©s et le courage de nous attaquer aux causes profondes de ces Ã©vÃ©nements naturels dÃ©vastateurs, avec, Ã  ce titre, un plan dâadaptation au changement climatique, qui sera trÃ¨s bientÃ´t prÃ©sentÃ© par le commissaire WopkeÂ Hoekstra.

Honourable Members, the EU, with the key support of the Parliament, has already made significant progress in strengthening wildfire preparedness in recent years. That work gives us a solid foundation to build on. We do not take this challenge lightly, especially as climate change accelerates and environmental degradation deepens. This is what European solidarity looks like in practice: working together to build a common collective preparedness for wildfires.

Je terminerai par une pensÃ©e pour tous ceux qui sont sur le front des incendies, qui combattent ensemble lâintensitÃ© des feux, Ã  quelques kilomÃ¨tres Ã  peine dâici, en dÃ©passant les frontiÃ¨res Ã  la fois de la langue, de la nationalitÃ© et de la culture, unis par la seule volontÃ© dâaider, de sauver des vies. Câest lâUnion europÃ©enne comme on lâaime. Câest lâUnion europÃ©enne de la solidaritÃ© en action.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marie Bjerre, President-in-Office of the Council. â MrÂ President, honourable Members, Commissioner, thank you for inviting me to this debate on such an important topic, also for our presidency.

I noted your stories and comments about fires, floods, extreme heat, and that illustrates the scale of the challenges. And it also points to the need to work for high climate ambitions. In this regard, I want to emphasise that the 2040 climate target is a priority for the Danish Presidency.

The Preparedness Union strategy also emphasises the societal dimension of preparedness. Some Member States and the EU institutions have already developed interesting actions which we can build upon. Engaging with our citizens on preparedness requires better communication and exchanges with them about the risks that they are facing, including those posed by extreme weather, such as wildfires and droughts, and what they can do to prepare themselves.

The European Parliament is in a good position to participate fully in the communication with citizens about the challenges of preparedness. The Council, therefore, counts on you for this common effort.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â

14. Presentation of the Stockpiling Strategies - strengthening response capacities for a changing risk and threat landscape (debate)
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â PredsedajÃºci . â ÄalÅ¡Ã­m bodom programu je vyhlÃ¡senie Komisie o prezentÃ¡cii stratÃ©giÃ­ pre vytvÃ¡ranie zÃ¡sob â posilnenie kapacÃ­t reakcie vzhÄ¾adom na meniacu sa panorÃ¡mu rizÃ­k a hrozieb [2025/2790(RSP)].

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Hadja Lahbib, Member of the Commission. â MrÂ President, honourable Members, today we are strengthening Europe's resilience with two new strategies on stockpiling and medical countermeasures. The threats confronting us today are no longer distant. Hybrid attacks, power outages, extreme weather, spreading diseases â these risks are real and they are already part of our daily reality across Europe. That is why we are reinforcing our preparedness.

A few months ago, we launched our new EU preparedness strategy, and today we are already putting it into action with these two deliverables. Since the start of my mandate, I have travelled across Europe and beyond to see how different countries prepare for emergencies. And everywhere I go, one thing is clear: when people are prepared, they feel more confident and more in control, they are not afraid.

I witnessed this most clearly in Sweden, where preparedness is part of citizens' DNA. From a young age, children learn what to do in an emergency situation, and adults have the tools and knowledge they need to act.

The lesson is very simple: the more we prepare, the less we fear. You would not get in your car without putting on your seatbelt. Preparedness works the same way: it gives us security before the impact. And that is what we are doing for Europe now with these two new strategies.

Allow me to start with the stockpiling strategy. This is the first time the European Union is adopting a truly common approach to stockpiling. Our goal is clear: to make sure that essential supplies are available when and where they are needed. We will do this through better planning, close coordination and taking shared ownership. We must move away from a fragmented reactive model and shift towards a proactive, coordinated approach.

This strategy covers the full stockpiling cycle, from A to Z. It starts with anticipating risks and identifying gaps, then it moves to pooling our efforts so we don't duplicate resources. We do not need fleets of Canada's aircraft in every Member State. Rather, we need a system that allows these resources to be quickly deployed across borders wherever they are needed most. With smarter coordination, we can be more efficient, more effective, and we can make better use of public money.

This also means strengthening transport and logistics and boosting civil military cooperation â something that has proven vital in both the pandemic and during natural disasters, like floods and wildfires, and fostering cooperation in external action and international partnership.

We will also make the private sector a strong partner in keeping our societies safe. We will work with European businesses in all Member States to ensure that essential items, from food and water to medicines and masks, are available and ready.

We are also creating a new EU stockpiling network to improve coordination between Member States and, of course, with EU institutions. This new network will bring together, for the first time, all national stockpiling authorities to improve coordination, exchange best practices and build the trust that is so essential in times of crisis. Because trust is what holds everything together and transparency is what helps us know where we stand, what we have, what we lack, and what we need to strengthen.

Material preparedness is about making sure the right supplies are in the right place at the right time. Some areas, like medical countermeasures, require closer attention because they are particularly complex to stockpile. But to stockpile a vaccine or a treatment, it first needs, of course, to exist. And this is where the medical countermeasure strategy comes in â our second major initiative.

Health preparedness is not a one-time effort, but a continuous commitment. And today we are making that commitment by presenting our medical countermeasures strategy. Medical countermeasures, such as vaccines, therapeutics, diagnostics and protective equipment and strategic assets, their rapid development, production and supply save lives, protect our frontline responders and help keep our societies running. Yet, these assets remain scarce or even unavailable for many health threats affecting Europe.

This medical countermeasures strategy has five key goals. First, to enhance our threat intelligence system. Detecting emerging threats early is key; it helps us identify the right medical countermeasure. That is why we will work with the European Centre for Disease Prevention and Control, the ECDC, to build an EU wastewater surveillance system and to expand it globally later on. It will act like an early warning radar for detecting infectious diseases in communities before symptoms appear at population level. This gives us valuable time to respond early.

We will also invest in next-generation mobile detection labs to reach the most remote and hardest hit areas, and to protect the most vulnerable.

Second, we will put forward measures to speed up the development of medical countermeasures. This includes launching a medical countermeasures accelerator and increasing the budget for HERA Invest. This will drive innovation and strengthen the competitiveness of a sector that is vital to our preparedness and also our security.

Third, we will work closely with the private sector and create a rapid and agile manufacturing partnership to boost the production of medical countermeasures â this is what we call 'RAMP-UP'.

Fourth, we will improve how we coordinate medical stockpiles across the EU and make joint procurement faster and more flexible so that we can respond quickly based on the specific threat we are facing.

Finally, to keep everyone safe, we need everyone on board. That is why we will deepen our civilian and military coordination, enhance our partnerships with global partners, and most importantly, invest in our greatest asset â our people. Researchers, manufacturers, doctors, nurses and caregivers â they are the heart of our preparedness strategy. They need the right skills and expertise to respond to today's health challenges.

Strong preparedness also requires investment, of course, but the cost of inaction is far greater. The pandemic showed us the price we payÂ when we are unprepared.

Honourable Members, with this medical countermeasures strategy, we are making a clear promise to all people: when the next crisis hits, Europe will be ready to act quickly and to save lives.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Kolegice i kolege, danas objavljene strategije i zakon o kritiÄnim lijekovima predstavljaju konkretan korak prema jaÄanju solidarnosti u Europskoj uniji, a nju moramo oÄuvati jer bez solidarnosti nema ni istinske Europske unije, i svakako pozdravljam donoÅ¡enje ovih strategija.Â Ono Å¡to nam malo tu fali su konkretne, naravno, odredbe o financiranju, ali nadam se da Äe se i taj dio nadopuniti, isto u kontekstu donoÅ¡enja novog viÅ¡egodiÅ¡njeg financijskog okvira, a vjerujem da Äemo i s izmjenama, koje planiramo vezano uz prijedlog zakona o kritiÄnim lijekovima, stvoriti jedan jedinstveni sustav koji Äe omoguÄiti da se svi Europljani osjeÄaju sigurno u sluÄaju kriza.

Â Â MirosÅawa Nykiel (PPE). â Panie PrzewodniczÄcy! Pani Komisarz!
