Â 

16. Democratic Republic of the Congo-Rwanda peace deal agreement (debate)
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â PredsedajÃºci . â ÄalÅ¡Ã­m bodom programu sÃº vyhlÃ¡senia Rady a Komisie o mierovej dohode medzi KonÅ¾skou demokratickou republikou a Rwandou [2025/2792(RSP)].

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Jozef SÃ­kela, Member of the Commission. â Mr President, honourable Members of the European Parliament, the peace agreement between the DRC and Rwanda, facilitated by the US, is a source of hope for the Great Lakes.

We welcome this positive step and stress how the parties to this agreement, all other regional players and actors like the EU need to work together for this accord to become the starting point of, first, a rapid relief to the dire humanitarian and respect for human rights situation in DRC. An estimated 3.8 million internally displaced persons (IDPs) in North and South Kivu live in precarious conditions, 6.4 million throughout the country, most of whom in the east.

And second, a longâterm positive dynamic in favour of peace and security. There are multiple components of the peace agreement, which relate to domains where the EU has already been active, and where we can thus offer a tangible, significant support to its implementation. Politically, but also in terms of economic integration and humanitarian assistance.

In terms of economic integration, the agreement foresees a separate accord on regional economic integration that is currently being negotiated. It should enable the parties to expand foreign trade and investment and introduce greater transparency to critical mineral supply chains. Reducing illicit economic pathways would benefit local populations rather than the armed groups, organised crime networks or corruption.

The EU has long been advocating for sustained integration across the Great Lakes. Our own experience in building a single market and reaping its security and prosperity benefits puts us in a unique position to share experiences and lessons learned. This was discussed with both Ministers of Foreign Affairs of Congo and Rwanda during the EUâAfrican Union ministerial meeting on 21 May 2025. We have been supporting integration efforts at regional, trilateral and bilateral level. In line with our Great Lakes strategy, our global gateway investments are aimed at fully harnessing the economic and political transformative potential across the entire region.

Just to mention a few examples of ongoing bilateral and regional actions which we can intensify and recalibrate and we can point to our support to.

Hydropower in the DRC, Rwanda and Burundi border area, all three countries being benefited by the construction of the Ruzizi III power plant. Joint management of water resources in Lakes Kivu and Tanganyika, pooling the management of natural resources in the Virunga National Park. Cross-border infrastructure to facilitate legitimate trade and movements of people. Support to improved traceability of raw materials, including minerals, replacing trafficking by trade.

In terms of humanitarian situation, we welcome the much-needed attention given to the situation of the people in eastern DRC and across the Great Lakes. EU humanitarian aid to DRC, with a focus on the East has been significant. EUR 112Â 000Â 000 in 2024 and EUR 100Â 000Â 000 in 2025 so far, but the needs are enormous and only partially met.

The European Union urges the parties to rapidly deliver on their commitments towards refugees and internally displaced persons. We stress the importance of ensuring the necessary conditions for the delivery of emergency relief and for the free, safe and unconditional access by humanitarian operators to vulnerable populations.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Ingeborg Ter Laak, namens de PPE-Fractie. â Voorzitter, commissaris, collegaâs, de vredesdeal van Congo en Rwanda leidt hopelijk tot het verminderen van de gevechten in de DRC, waar inmiddels duizenden mensen vermoord zijn en nog eens honderdduizenden gevlucht. Deze gevechten moeten echt stoppen. Binnen de vredesdeal is er ruimte voor plannen om Congolese mineralen te gaan verhandelen, mogelijk met de VS als investeerder. Deze mijnen zouden komen naast de mijnen van bijvoorbeeld al de Chinezen en de Russen die daar ook actief zijn. Het belangrijkste met deze metalen is dat de economische welvaart terugkomt bij de Congolezen zelf. Het land moet niet leeggeroofd worden door buitenlandse inmenging. De EU lijkt aan de zijkant te hebben gestaan bij het maken van deze deal. Daarbij is het belangrijk dat de EU nu de situatie blijft monitoren en kijkt hoe Europa met Congo als rechtmatige eigenaar van de kritieke materialen, een partnerschap kan afsluiten met voordelen voor zowel Congo als Europa.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marit Maij, namens de S&D-Fractie. â Voorzitter, ik ontmoette haar vorige maand in Congo, een meisje van vijftien. Thuis verkrachtte een bendelid haar zo bruut dat ze in het ziekenhuis geholpen en gehecht moest worden. Ze wilde graag zo snel mogelijk terug naar huis om weer naar school te gaan. Want wiskunde, dat vindt ze het leukst. Maar ze durft niet meer naar huis. Ze vraagt mij of ik kinderen heb. Ja, ik heb twee kinderen. Zij wil later geen kinderen meer, want ze vertrouwt mannen niet. Ze weet niet of ze hen ooit nog kan vertrouwen.

Seksueel geweld tekent voor het leven. Toch rept dit vredesakkoord met geen woord over rechtvaardigheid en restitutie voor slachtoffers in het conflict. Dat er een vredesakkoord ligt, is een belangrijke stap, maar het richt zich niet op de oorzaken van het conflict: de economische ongelijkheid, de illegale mijnbouw en ook de verantwoordelijkheid van M23.

Er moet meer humanitaire hulp komen, vooral voor de slachtoffers van het seksueel geweld. De internationale gemeenschap moet zich ook richten op de oorzaken van het conflict en de daders van oorlogsmisdaden vervolgen en berechten.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Philippe Olivier, au nom du groupe PfE. â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, le rÃ©cent accord entre le Rwanda et la RÃ©publique dÃ©mocratique du Congo, certes fragile mais bienvenu, vient souligner lâincurie de lâUnion europÃ©enne dans cette guerre Ã©pouvantable. Incurie, parce que vous avez dÃ©sarmÃ© diplomatiquement des pays membres comme la France ou la Belgique qui, pour des raisons historiques ou linguistiques, Ã©taient lÃ©gitimes Ã  agir. Vous les avez neutralisÃ©s sans rien faire de votre cÃ´tÃ©. Incurie, parce que vous avez laissÃ© le Rwanda, pays agresseur, receler les matiÃ¨res premiÃ¨res volÃ©es Ã  la RDC. Le cessez-le-feu arrachÃ© par les Ãtats-Unis de DonaldÂ Trump vous oblige Ã  imposer au Rwanda le dÃ©part effectif de ses troupes du territoire congolais, le dÃ©sarmement total de la milice criminelleÂ M23 et lâarrÃªt du pillage des richesses congolaises.

Il y a Ã©galement la question des rÃ©fugiÃ©s intÃ©rieurs du Congo, dont lâUnion europÃ©enne doit se prÃ©occuper, comme elle sâintÃ©resse Ã  juste titre au sort des populations civiles dans les autres guerres et, ce matin encore, Ã  la population civile de lâUkraine.

Enfin, vous avez le devoir dâÃ©tablir, au bÃ©nÃ©fice des deux parties, des relations commerciales avec ce grand pays quâest le Congo. Lâaccord qui vient dâÃªtre signÃ© nous appelle Ã  lâaction. Jâai cru comprendre que la Commission voulait agir: nous verrons bien ce que valent ces paroles.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Nicolas Bay, au nom du groupe ECR. â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, le 27Â juin, Donald Trump a rÃ©ussi lÃ  oÃ¹ lâUnion europÃ©enne a Ã©chouÃ©, en prÃ©sidant un accord de paix entre le Rwanda et la RÃ©publique dÃ©mocratique du Congo. Nous nous en rÃ©jouissons, bien sÃ»r. Les carnages qui se dÃ©roulent dans les provinces de lâest de la RDC dÃ©stabilisent profondÃ©ment toute une rÃ©gion. Les crimes du M23, soutenus par Kagame, et les abjections islamistes du groupe de lâAlliance des forces dÃ©mocratiques nâont pas disparu. Depuis lâoffensive de la milice pro-rwandaise, 7Â 000Â morts et 500Â 000Â dÃ©placÃ©s sont Ã  dÃ©plorer.

Toutefois il existe un espoir pour que la souverainetÃ© de Kinshasa cesse dâÃªtre malmenÃ©e au Kivu et que les Congolais cessent dâÃªtre les victimes expiatoires de la repentance coloniale europÃ©enne face Ã  Kigali. Je ne peux que partager avec vous mon Ã©motion en pensant Ã  toutes les victimes de lâÃ©glise de Kasanga, dÃ©capitÃ©es parce que chrÃ©tiennes, en fÃ©vrier dernier, par les porte-machettes de Daech en Afrique centrale.

Alors que lâUnion europÃ©enne avait tolÃ©rÃ© le pillage des ressources de nombreuses provinces congolaises par leur voisin, il ne faut cependant pas Ãªtre dupe dâune autre rÃ©alitÃ©. Câest bien pour concurrencer la Chine, bÃ©nÃ©ficiaire dâun contrat historique au Congo, que lâadministration amÃ©ricaine vient de se mobiliser. Câest donc parce que lâUnion europÃ©enne a Ã©tÃ© inefficace quâelle ne bÃ©nÃ©ficiera dâaucune retombÃ©e positive.

LâEurope Ã©change avec lâAfrique des aides financiÃ¨res contre des migrants au lieu de saisir des occasions sur le plan Ã©conomique. La RÃ©publique dÃ©mocratique du Congo, deuxiÃ¨me pays francophone du monde, doit Ãªtre un partenaire de poids pour lâinfluence europÃ©enne en Afrique. Cessons notre aveuglement devant le Rwanda, et cela redeviendra possible.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Hilde Vautmans, au nom du groupe Renew. â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, chers collÃ¨gues, nous saluons lâÃ©tape importante qui est celle de la recherche de la paix durable au Congo. Mais est-ce que cet accord amÃ©liore la situation sur le terrain?

Monsieur le Commissaire, oÃ¹ est lâEurope? Pourquoi leÂ mÃ©morandum d'entente avec le Rwanda nâest-il pas encore suspendu? Ce conflit a fauchÃ© plus de 10Â millions de vies. Câest comme si un pays tout entier, tel que la Belgique, avait Ã©tÃ© rayÃ© de la carte.

Dans le cadre de notre mission, en mai, nous avons constatÃ© que le peuple congolais, aprÃ¨s plus de trente annÃ©es de conflits orchestrÃ©s par le Rwanda et consorts, aspirait avant tout Ã  la justice, qui est le pilier essentiel dâune paix durable.

CollÃ¨gues, le Conseil et la Commission doivent maintenir la pression par des sanctions, car, malgrÃ© la signature de lâaccord de paix, le Rwanda continue de menacer et de conquÃ©rir dâautres territoires. Prenez les sanctions qui sâimposent et garantissez que les responsables de ces atrocitÃ©s ne resteront pas impunis, ainsi que lâa exigÃ© le Parlement europÃ©en dans ses deux rÃ©solutions. Amani kwenu!

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Mounir Satouri, au nom du groupe Verts/ALE. â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, alors que nous dÃ©battons de lâaccord de paix entre la RDC et le Rwanda, je veux dâabord rappeler aux Congolais quâils me trouveront toujours du cÃ´tÃ© de la paix, Ã  me rÃ©jouir quand les armes se taisent et quand le sang cesse de couler. Mais ils ne me trouveront jamais du cÃ´tÃ© de ceux qui bradent leur souverainetÃ© et leurs richesses.Â Åuvrer pour la paix, câest avant tout Åuvrer pour la justice.

Or, DonaldÂ Trump poursuit la mÃªme logique impÃ©rialiste quâen Ukraine: aussitÃ´t lâaccord signÃ©, il se fÃ©licite dâavoir obtenu une grande partie des droits miniers en RDC. Cet accord ne garantit pas la paix durable, il ne reconnaÃ®t pas la responsabilitÃ© de lâagresseur rwandais et nâexige aucune rÃ©forme de la part du gouvernement congolais. Cet accord ne fait que garantir la mainmise Ã©trangÃ¨re sur les ressources de la RDC.

Cet accord est aussi lâÃ©chec de lâUnion europÃ©enne, qui aurait dÃ» incarner une voie diffÃ©rente pour la paix: celle de la justice. Câest votre Ã©chec, monsieurÂ SÃ­kela, vous qui, il y a encore quelques mois, affirmiez ne pas Ãªtre au courant des agissements du Rwanda en RDC. Il est temps dâagir: cessez de ne rien faire ou dâÃªtre complice!

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marc Botenga, au nom du groupe The Left. â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, Monsieur le Commissaire, la paix, câest bien. On applaudit la paix. Mais vous avez oubliÃ© une chose: quâa dit Trump, en fait, aux Congolais? Il leur a dit: Â«Si vous voulez la paix, va falloir me filer vos richesses.Â» Il impose donc unÂ deal aux Congolais, lesquels lui offrent le contrÃ´le des minerais. Câest ce quâon appelle du racket, tout simplement.

LâOccident âÂ lâEurope aussi, dâailleursÂ â a dâabord Â«sponsorisÃ©Â» le Rwanda, les rebelles et la guerre Ã  lâEst pour piller les minerais du Congo. Ce sont les mÃªmes qui, ensuite, viennent proposer la paix des voleurs et ramÃ¨nent des mercenaires amÃ©ricains pour contrÃ´ler les mines des Congolais. En Ukraine, câest la mÃªme chose âÂ Â«Vous voulez un soutien? Ãcoutez, vous allez nous cÃ©der vos matiÃ¨res premiÃ¨resÂ»Â â, et au Moyen-Orient aussi, dâailleurs âÂ de la Libye Ã  lâIran en passant par lâIrak, tout pays qui veut contrÃ´ler son gaz et son pÃ©trole se fait attaquer, se fait bombarder. MÃªme les Palestiniens, aujourdâhui, se font dÃ©gager pour que leur gaz puisse Ãªtre volÃ©. Câest Ã§a lâimpÃ©rialisme. On le sait trÃ¨s bien: lâAfrique nâest pas pauvre, lâAfrique est appauvrie âÂ et cela crÃ©e aujourdâhui de la colÃ¨re.

Si lâEurope ne change pas dâapproche, nous serons chassÃ©s avec du goudron et des plumes par les Congolais, par les Africains. Notre responsabilitÃ© est de ne plus suivre cette politique impÃ©rialiste, celle que nous avons suivie en soutenant les rebelles, le Rwanda et dâautres impÃ©rialistes comme les Ãtats-Unis.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Wouter Beke (PPE). â Voorzitter, commissaris, collegaâs, begin dit jaar geleden laaide het geweld in Oost-Congo opnieuw gruwelijk op. We zagen de gruwelijke beelden op televisie. Maar eigenlijk zien we al dertig jaar deze gruwelijke beelden op televisie. Nu is er een vredesdeal getekend. Dat is op zich een goede stap, maar toch ook een stap met een aantal vragen.

De eerste vraag, mijnheer de commissaris, was: waar is Europa in dit conflict? Waar zaten wij? Was dit een vredesdeal of was dit een mineralendeal en zal op het eind van de rit alleen nog de mineralendeal overblijven en zal de vredesdeal opnieuw verdwijnen? Ik denk dat dat een cruciale vraag is. Een vraag die wij als Europa misschien nog in de juiste richting kunnen duwen door ervoor te zorgen dat we in de toekomst wel mee aan die tafel gaan zitten. Want daar is wel een vrede getekend, maar een aantal cruciale spelers, collegaâs, geven niet thuis. De rebellengroep M23 zegt: wij voelen ons niet gehouden en gebonden aan deze vredesdeal. Terwijl dat dan natuurlijk wel de essentie van de zaak is.

Voorzitter, commissaris, collegaâs, ik roep iedereen op en ik roep u ook op. Ik roep de Europese Unie op. Ik roep de hoge commissaris, de hoge vertegenwoordiger op om samen meer aandacht te besteden aan dit conflict. Een conflict dat al decennialang woedt, een conflictÂ dat al miljoenen doden heeft gevraagd, een conflict - u heeft dat zelf al aangehaald - dat 2,5Â miljoen intern verplaatsten, alleen al in die regio, met zich meebrengt.

Er is vandaag een vredesdeal. Dat kan positief uitpakken. Zorg ervoor dat het geen mineralendeal wordt zoals in OekraÃ¯ne. Dat kunnen we alleen maar doen door zelf als Europa mee aan tafel te zitten, omdat het in ons belang is.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Francisco Assis (S&D). â Senhor Presidente, Senhor ComissÃ¡rio, Ã  primeira vista, este acordo de paz atÃ© parece uma notÃ­cia relativamente animadora no atual panorama do continente africano. Trataâse, no fundo, de uma transaÃ§Ã£o entre estes dois paÃ­ses aqui referidos e os Estados Unidos da AmÃ©rica: o cessarâfogo e minerais em troca de investimento.

Contudo, a responsabilizaÃ§Ã£o e a reparaÃ§Ã£o pelas gravÃ­ssimas violaÃ§Ãµes dos direitos humanos, cometidas por ambas as partes, ficaram de fora deste acordo. Este acordo trarÃ¡ a paz, mas manterÃ¡ a impunidade. A conflitualidade nÃ£o vai desaparecer, apenas ficarÃ¡ oportunisticamente suspensa, o que significa que, a qualquer momento, poderÃ¡ ser oportunisticamente reacendida.

Por isso mesmo, a UniÃ£o Europeia nÃ£o deve remeterâse a um papel de mera observadora. Deve esforÃ§arâse por tratar a raiz da instabilidade e da violÃªncia nesta regiÃ£o e tem de colocar a proteÃ§Ã£o da populaÃ§Ã£o civil acima de tudo, condicionando as suas relaÃ§Ãµes com o Ruanda. Desde logo, a retirada completa das suas tropas e milÃ­cias afiliadas da RepÃºblica DemocrÃ¡tica do Congo e suspendendo, assim, o Memorando de Entendimento com esse paÃ­s enquanto isso nÃ£o suceder.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

PREÈEDINÈIA: VICTOR NEGRESCU
VicepreÈedinte

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â France Jamet (PfE). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, oui, lâaccord de paix nÃ©gociÃ© Ã  Washington entre le Rwanda et la RÃ©publique dÃ©mocratique du Congo sous lâÃ©gide des Ãtats-Unis est dramatique Ã  plus dâun titre. Dâabord, parce quâil tord le bras au Congo en lâengageant Ã  entÃ©riner les souffrances de son peuple et quâil soutient le dictateur rwandais Kagame. Ensuite, parce quâil infÃ©ode lâUnion europÃ©enne aux quatre volontÃ©s des Ãtats-Unis, nous mettant hors-jeu dans une rÃ©gion cruciale pour nos intÃ©rÃªts stratÃ©giques.

Pourquoi? Comment? Parce qu'aprÃ¨s trente ans dâagressions et dâexactions rwandaises et 6Â millions de morts congolais, lâUnion europÃ©enne a regardÃ© ailleurs, en se cachant derriÃ¨re une indignation Ã  deux vitesses et en soutenant une puissance agressive et invasive vis-Ã -vis de son voisin âÂ situation quâelle est pourtant si prompte Ã  dÃ©noncer ailleurs.

Alors, de cet accord et de ces trente ans de dÃ©mission on retiendra simplement que rien nâest rÃ©glÃ©. Autrement dit: tout reste Ã  faire.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Jan-Christoph Oetjen (Renew). â Herr PrÃ¤sident, verehrte Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Gut, dass der Krieg zu Ende geht. Gut, dass es ein Ende hat, dass die Milizen der M23 aus Ruanda im Kongo operieren. Gut, dass Ruanda aufhÃ¶rt, die Ressourcen des Kongo zu plÃ¼ndern. Alles gut also? Nein. In Ruanda wird weiter die Opposition unterdrÃ¼ckt. Wir haben keine freie MeinungsÃ¤uÃerung in Ruanda.

Und ich mÃ¶chte darauf hinweisen, dass am 19.Â Juni die OppositionsfÃ¼hrerin in Ruanda, Victoire Ingabire, verhaftet wurde. Und gestern gab es die erste Verhandlung ihres Gerichtsprozesses. Ein politischer Gerichtsprozess, wo ihr vorgeworfen wird, eine kriminelle Vereinigung gegrÃ¼ndet zu haben, und es ist unklar, ob sie aus dem GefÃ¤ngnis wieder freikommt, verehrte Damen und Herren.

Und ich mÃ¶chte an dieser Stelle einmal sagen: Die Kommission muss hier tÃ¤tig werden, weiter am Ball bleiben, dafÃ¼r sorgen, dass Menschenrechte, dass Freiheit, dass Grundrechte auch in Ruanda durchgesetzt werden. Und gerade der Fall von Victoire Ingabire sollte von unserer Kommission dort bei der Regierung angesprochen werden.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Pernando Barrena Arza (The Left). â SeÃ±or presidente, desde finales de la dÃ©cada de 1990, el este de la RepÃºblica DemocrÃ¡tica del Congo ha sido escenario de conflictos interconectados marcados por la presencia de mÃºltiples grupos armados, la disputa por el control de recursos naturales estratÃ©gicos y tambiÃ©n las consecuencias del genocidio del 1994 en Ruanda.Â MÃ¡s de siete millones de personas se encuentran desplazadas internamente en la RepÃºblica DemocrÃ¡tica del Congo y las violaciones sistemÃ¡ticas del Derecho internacional humanitario son mÃ¡s que habituales.

En este contexto, el pasado 27 de junio se firmÃ³ entre la RepÃºblica DemocrÃ¡tica del Congo y Ruanda un acuerdo de paz que prevÃ© la retirada de tropas ruandesas del territorio congoleÃ±o, la desmovilizaciÃ³n del M23 y garantÃ­as para el retorno seguro de las personas desplazadas. A pesar del escepticismo sobre su implementaciÃ³n, este acuerdo es una oportunidad relevante para la desescalada del conflicto y la creaciÃ³n de condiciones para una paz sostenible. Paralelamente, la MisiÃ³n de las Naciones Unidas se retira a lo largo de este aÃ±o y deja un vacÃ­o en una regiÃ³n con altÃ­simos niveles de violencia e inestabilidad.

La UniÃ³n debe estar a disposiciÃ³n de las autoridades congoleÃ±as para que puedan asumir plenamente las funciones de seguridad y protecciÃ³n de civiles. AdemÃ¡s de apoyar los tÃ©rminos del acuerdo de paz, la UniÃ³n Europea debe alejarse de visiones neocoloniales y extractivas que estÃ¡n en el origen del conflicto.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Jan FarskÃ½ (PPE). â Mr President, do you use a cell phone? I'm sure you do. Most certainly you will find parts in it which come from Congo, from a country that is consumed by a conflict for more than a decade. Rwanda-supported militias took over the Congolese strategic areas. Every four minutes, a woman was raped â every four minutes.

It seems now that we have a deal between Congo and Rwanda. It should be an opportunity for Europe. Africa has its own experience with Russian occupation and modern Chinese feudalism. Europe, on the other hand, offers a partnership. I've been to Congo and I can confirm that Europe is seen as a solid partner.

The European initiative â the Global Gateway â is based on cooperation, not dependence. As a shadow rapporteur, I'm glad I can shape how the Global Gateway works.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Hannes Heide (S&D). â Herr PrÃ¤sident, Herr Kommissar! Es gibt nichts, was unsere Welt mehr braucht als Frieden an einem der grÃ¶Ãten Krisenherde und KriegsschauplÃ¤tze unserer Zeit. RegelmÃ¤Ãig haben wir im EuropÃ¤ischen Parlament die unertrÃ¤gliche Lage fÃ¼r die Menschen, Gewalt und Missbrauch im Ostkongo thematisiert. Das Abkommen zwischen Ruanda und der Demokratischen Republik Kongo kann ein erster wichtiger Schritt hin zu Frieden und StabilitÃ¤t sein. Entscheidend ist allerdings, wie das Abkommen tatsÃ¤chlich umgesetzt wird, ob es den Ursachen des Konflikts entgegenwirken kann. Die UnterstÃ¼tzung der M23âRebellen durch Ruanda muss beendet werden. Ihre Ausklammerung aus dem Abkommen ist ein VersÃ¤umnis. Der Handel mit Rohstoffen darf nicht lÃ¤nger Korruption und Konflikte befeuern. Es stimmt, in jedem unserer Handys ist ein StÃ¼ck KongoâKobalt drinnen. WÃ¤hrend die USâAdministration vor allem wirtschaftliche Interessen verfolgt, mÃ¼ssen humanitÃ¤re BedÃ¼rfnisse im Vordergrund stehen. Langfristige StabilitÃ¤t entsteht nur dort, wo Perspektiven wachsen. Das schÃ¼tzt am Ende auch Europas Sicherheit und GlaubwÃ¼rdigkeit.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

IntervenÈii la cerere

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Juan Fernando LÃ³pez Aguilar (S&D). â SeÃ±or presidente, el pasado mes de abril tuve el honor de ser parte de una misiÃ³n del Parlamento Europeo âde la Asamblea Parlamentaria Ãfrica-UE, de la que soy miembroâ a la RepÃºblica DemocrÃ¡tica del Congo.

Nos entrevistamos largamente con el presidente de la RepÃºblica, FÃ©lix Tshisekedi, y creo que no revelo ningÃºn secreto si digo que toda su intervenciÃ³n se centrÃ³ en la preocupaciÃ³n con respecto del conflicto con Ruanda, que desde hace dÃ©cadas gobierna Paul Kagame, y la intervenciÃ³n de M23 en Goma y en regiones de Congo ricas en recursos naturales.

La intercesiÃ³n de Estados Unidos y Qatar a travÃ©s de la penetraciÃ³n de esos recursos naturales âel litio, el cobalto, las tierras rarasâ en Congo puede ser celebrada, sÃ­, como un acuerdo de paz que hacÃ­a falta para que cesara el derramamiento de sangre, pero en ningÃºn caso condona la responsabilidad penal de las masivas violaciones de derechos humanos. Mujeres, niÃ±os vulnerables, que han pagado con su vida ese conflicto, y esas responsabilidades penales en ningÃºn caso pueden quedar disueltas en el acuerdo de paz. Esa es la vulnerabilidad del acuerdo.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

(Ãncheierea intervenÈiilor la cerere)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Jozef SÃ­kela, Member of the Commission. â Mr President, honourable Members of the European Parliament, before I jump to my closing remarks, please allow me to at least partially answer some of your questions. Mainly, where is my role or the Commission's role in the whole negotiation process? Unfortunately, I am meanwhile missing the most outspoken people here. Okay.

Who helped facilitate the deal was a matter for the two parties to decide, not for the EU or anybody else to determine. The EU's overarching goal is that a peace agreement be reached and respected. We commend the US for having facilitated this. Our strong, principled stance in last February, March, was part of the pressure that drew the parties â both parties â to the negotiation table. The EU, and in particular the EUSR, the Special Representative for the Great Lakes region, has been in regular contact with all key partners.

On the MOU on sustainable raw materials value chain: the EU condemns the illegal exploitation and trafficking of natural resources from the DRC, regardless if it is done by Rwandese or some of the Congolese. Let me be very open. The effective certification of supply chain, regional cooperation and trade are crucial steps in this regard. For this reason, the EU signed the MOUs with both DRC and Rwanda. The MOU with Rwanda in particular provides a platform to engage Rwanda on the transparency and traceability of mineral value chains, and to encourage Rwanda to join the Extractive Industry Transparency Initiative.

Legitimate regional trade and cooperation on natural resources can be important drivers of economic development, creating win-win-win solutions through the Great Lakes regions. We are concerned with ending the illegal trafficking of Congolese resources via Rwanda. Discussions with the Member States are ongoing and the MOU in question is now under review.

The peace agreement can bring hope to many and is undoubtedly a step in the right direction â we hope the first of many. We are not naÃ¯ve and do recognise the many challenges that lie ahead. The tragic history of the Great Lakes, which has witnessed multiple failed attempts at solving this crisis in eastern DRC, calls for caution and prudence. But for now, this is the best chance we have to help build a momentum for peace. The region cannot afford to miss this opportunity.

The implementation of the peace agreement, the successful and satisfactory conclusion of the talks between the DRC and M23, and the coordination and continuation of the other peace initiatives will not be easy and will require coordinated and concerted efforts by all stakeholders. The EU is ready to do its part to contribute to this effort, drawing lessons from the past, capitalising on its existing actions, but ready to do more.

Thank you very much for your attention and for the balanced debate.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â President. â Thank you, Commissioner. I can assure you that the debate is important for the Parliament. Many of the colleagues are at the group level right now discussing important issues related also to the Commission.

The debate is closed.

DeclaraÈii scrise (articolul 178)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 		Â 

Â Â Marcin Sypniewski (ESN), na piÅmie. â Åwiat toczy dziÅ brutalnÄ grÄ o zasoby â i Europa z niej odpada. Konflikt miÄdzy DRK a RwandÄ to nie tylko napiÄcia etniczne, ale bezwzglÄdna walka o kobalt, tantal, lit, zÅoto â surowce niezbÄdne do zielonej transformacji Zachodu.

Unia Europejska podpisaÅa z RwandÄ umowÄ o tych surowcach, choÄ wie, Å¼e pochodzÄ one z terenÃ³w Konga kontrolowanych przez rebeliantÃ³w i wydobywanych rÄkami dzieci. A dziÅ stroi siÄ w piÃ³ra rozjemcy? Kogo prÃ³bujemy przekonaÄ?

Tymczasem przywÃ³dcy DRK â zainspirowani negocjacjami USA z UkrainÄ â zaproponowali Amerykanom dostÄp do zÅÃ³Å¼ w zamian za pomoc w zakoÅczeniu wojny. 27 czerwca w Waszyngtonie podpisano porozumienie pokojowe pod auspicjami USA, oparte na formule: bezpieczeÅstwo za mineraÅy. To element wiÄkszej ukÅadanki â amerykaÅskiej rywalizacji z Chinami o wpÅywy w Afryce.

RolÄ mediatora przejmuje dziÅ maÅy Katar, a my â pozbawieni wpÅywÃ³w â stoimy z boku. ÅmiejÄ siÄ z nas, bo sami â w imiÄ zielonego szaleÅstwa â niszczymy wÅasny przemysÅ, by potem kupowaÄ âzieloneâ produkty z krwawych surowcÃ³w, wytwarzane cudzymi rÄkami.

Europa musi siÄ wreszcie obudziÄ â jeÅli nie chce z eksportera stabilnoÅci staÄ siÄ importerem chaosu.

Â 

17. Outcome of the Conference on the Financing for Development in Seville (debate)
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â PreÅedinte. â UrmÄtorul punct de pe ordinea de zi este dezbaterea privind DeclaraÈiile Consiliului Èi Comisiei referitoare la rezultatele ConferinÈei privind finanÈarea pentru dezvoltare de la Sevilla (2025/2793(RSP)).

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marie Bjerre, President-in-Office of the Council. â MrÂ President, honourable Members, Commissioner, the fourth international conference on financing for development in Seville has marked a key moment for global cooperation. This comes at a time of historical challenges and urgent need for renewed commitment to the sustainable development goals.

The mere fact that the conference was hosted on European soil underscores the EU's unwavering commitment to multilateralism, solidarity and a rulesâbased international order. In Seville, the EU reaffirmed its unity, leadership and commitment to global sustainable development and the SDGs, working closely with partners to promote reforms and fair representation in international institutions.

The conference achieved consensus, a success in itself, on a comprehensive outcome document. It lays out an ambitious package of reforms and actions to close the USDÂ 40âtrillion annual financing gap for the SDGs. This outcome reaffirmed a number of key priorities promoted by the EU throughout the conference process: domestic resource mobilisation, international trade and private sector mobilisation, debt sustainability and further reform of the international financial architecture. Also, it commits to opening an international intergovernmental process at the UN to address gaps in the global debt architecture, with the aim of achieving more comprehensive and fair debt relief.

The EU's Global Gateway strategy and support for innovative financing mechanisms were key contributions to mobilising resources and supporting transformative development. As such, they helped EU contribute to the 2030 agenda.Â By investing in quality infrastructure projects, Global Gateway strengthened the resilience and autonomy of the EU's partners while supporting their urgent priorities, including climate action, energy and digital transition, health and education.

With many transformative investment projects, reflecting the needs and objectives of our partners in Africa, Latin America, Caribbean and Asia, the Global Gateway is an instrument to advance the sustainable economic and human development in these countries and build lasting mutually beneficial partnerships. Later this year, in November, the EU will hold summits with the Community of Latin American and Caribbean states, and with the African Union. These will be key occasions to further advance our development cooperation agenda with two of our most important regional cooperation partners.

Of course, Seville is not the end of the road, and there is still a long way to reach our common objective and achieving sustainable development by 2030. This agreement is not as perfect, but it provides a compass and a pathway for development finance in the upcoming decade. It also demonstrates that multilateralism can still deliver, even in a fractured global context. This is our only way forward. As the President of the European Council, AntÃ³nio Costa, said in his address to the Seville conference, it is only together that we can tackle climate change, poverty and inequalities, and build a lasting peace.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Jozef SÃ­kela, Member of the Commission. â MrÂ President, honourable Members of the European Parliament, thank you for inviting me to reflect on the outcome of the fourth International Financing for Development Conference in Seville. This was a rare multilateral moment â the first such global gathering on development finance in ten years.

Throughout the conference, Europe played a central role. Together with President von der Leyen and President Costa, we sent a strong political signal confirming Europe's development leadership. And our message was clear: the EU is fully committed to multilateralism and to finding common solutions to mobilise more financing for sustainable development.

Access to finance is among the top priorities of our partner countries. As the UN Secretary-General highlighted, financing is the engine of development. And the outcomes of the conference â the Seville Commitment and the Seville Platform for Action â now provide a good basis for action, while respecting the roles and mandates of international fora and organisations.

In my view, in Seville, we made progress on four important points. First, domestic resource mobilisation is key. It remains the most predictable source of financing for sustainable development. The discussions in Seville underlined the importance of building fair and effective tax systems and fighting tax avoidance, tax evasion and illicit financial flows.

But collecting more is not sufficient; enhancing spending efficiency is also essential to ensure that every country directs much-needed resources towards priorities such as education, health and social protection. The EU played a leading role in driving this agenda forward in Seville. We endorsed the Seville Declaration on Domestic Revenue Mobilisation and call it a Seville Platform initiative, encouraging others to provide double support to domestic revenue mobilisation.

Second, private sector mobilisation must be a part of our action. Public money alone will not be enough to bridge the annual EURÂ 4Â trillion gap to achieve the SDGs. We need to mobilise private investments at scale. With our global gateway strategy, we are ahead of the curve. We are already using public funds to leverage private investments towards sustainable development.

For instance, we offer guarantees to de-risk private investments in partner countries. We provide innovative financial instruments, including blended finance, green bonds and development venture capital funds. We also foster an enabling environment to encourage sustainable private investments.

Third, official development assistance remains essential for our international partnerships. We have not backed away from our commitments. In 2024, the EU and its Member States provided 42Â % of official development assistance globally. Now, this money needs to be used more efficiently and strategically. It is also essential to mobilise other sources of financing for sustainable development, and to promote both our priorities and those of our partners.

And finally, we must continue efforts to reform the international financial architecture to make it more inclusive, efficient and representative. We've made progress on different aspects over the last few years. Now, we must build on the momentum of the Financing for Development Conference to continue reforming multilateral development banks and increasing their lending capacity through the full implementation of the G20 roadmap; further enhance the representation and the voice of partner countries in decision-making; and enhance debt transparency and improve existing processes for debt treatment and debt relief, including efforts to provide liquidity to vulnerable countries while ensuring sustainable debt management.

Dear colleagues, there is one more message we should bring home from this conference: development must not be an ideological issue. It is not a 'left or right' topic. It is a forward-thinking strategic investment. The tools are already in our hands; we must use them to their full capacity to promote sustainable development and invest in global public goods.

I am looking forward to hearing your opinions and thoughts.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Lukas Mandl, on behalf of the PPE Group. â Mr President, I should like to thank the Commissioner for his courageous speech. I'm happy to have for the development policies and efforts of this Parliament such a champion in the European Commission when it comes to sustainable development. I am also happy that the European Parliament today managed to decide, in our voting, on a very particular specific message. Namely, we welcome the outcome of the fourth international conference on financing for development in Seville as a significant effort to reform the international financial architecture, readdress the cost of borrowing and scale up investment to close the financing gap for sustainable development.

The very clear message that we have taken today, and since there is a lot at stake when it comes to development, is that it's often about life and death, and it's about opportunities for generations because there's a lot at stake. I want to speak very parliamentarily as a parliamentarian today. We have decided on that today because we were not able to decide before Seville here in the European Parliament. And the reason was not Charles Goerens, who was the rapporteur for this file, whom I commend for his efforts and for his excellent work in the negotiations â very compromise-oriented, and this is what it should be about. But after these negotiations, this compromise was targeted by amendments. We didn't have a chance for majorities â nearly all of them, except one. And this hit. And this is why last time we couldn't decide on Seville.

This can be a lesson for all of us to stick together, because it's a lot at stake, as I have mentioned, to find positive compromises and to contribute to sustainable development in the best sense we can as the European Parliament.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marit Maij, namens de S&D-Fractie. â Voorzitter, commissaris, mevrouw de minister, vorige week gold code rood in het zuiden van Europa vanwege het warme weer. Maar voor meer dan honderd landen, die in vlammende schulden leven, is het al heel lang code rood. Wij waren in het hete Sevilla voor de internationale conferentie over ontwikkelingsfinanciering. Zonder standpunt van het Europees Parlement wel te verstaan, omdat de rechterflank hier in het Parlement het verslag eerst verzwakte en daarna ook nog wegstemde. En dan vervolgens ook applaudisseerde, terwijl in werkelijkheid miljoenen mensen overlijden omdat er minder hulp naar ontwikkelingslanden gaat, meer dan 700Â miljoen mensen in extreme armoede leven en dat aantal al decennia toeneemt. Honderd landen betalen meer aan schulden en rente dan zij beschikbaar hebben in hun begroting voor sociaal beleid, gezondheid en infrastructuur bij elkaar.

Het systeem is stuk. In 2025 zijn er wereldwijd een recordaantal van meer dan 3Â 000Â miljardairs. Dit systeem produceert elke keer meer miljardairs, terwijl er mensen ook sterven van de honger. Dit systeem is moreel corrupt en daarom is het zo pijnlijk dat dit Parlement geen standpunt heeft ingenomen en dat de Europese Commissie ook een sterretje heeft geplaatst bij de paragrafen over internationale schuldenverlichting en de hervorming van het internationale financiÃ«le systeem.

De volgende stap is nu aan de slag te gaan met de uitvoering van het slotdocument van de conferentie over ontwikkelingsfinanciering, en het internationale systeem, dat stuk is, te gaan repareren. Want we hebben nog steeds de kans om het te repareren en die kans moeten we niet laten lopen.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Jorge BuxadÃ© Villalba, en nombre del Grupo PfE. â SeÃ±or presidente, la Conferencia Internacional sobre la FinanciaciÃ³n para el Desarrollo de las Naciones Unidas en Sevilla mostrÃ³ la peor cara del globalismo y la peor cara de Pedro SÃ¡nchez, la cara mÃ¡s siniestra y mÃ¡s hipÃ³crita, mientras Pedro SÃ¡nchez pensaba cÃ³mo seguir desangrando con impuestos a las clases medias y trabajadoras para financiar proyectos demenciales como âleoâ Â«el fomento de las resistencias queer feministas interseccionales contra las polÃ­ticas antigÃ©nero transnacionalesÂ». Â¿Han entendido algo? Lo Ãºnico que entiendo es que los espaÃ±oles no pueden acceder a una vivienda digna.

Mientras Pedro SÃ¡nchez ha tenido a 7Â 000Â policÃ­as para garantizar la seguridad de los invitados, en EspaÃ±a la gente decente se tiene que organizar para defenderse en la calle. Mientras Pedro SÃ¡nchez proclamaba la estrategia global contra la corrupciÃ³n, Santos CerdÃ¡n, el secretario de organizaciÃ³n de su partido, ingresaba en la cÃ¡rcel de Soto del Real, investigado por organizaciÃ³n criminal, trÃ¡fico de influencias y cohecho. Mientras Von der Leyen hablaba de la arquitectura financiera internacional, decenas de empresas se han quedado sin crÃ©dito porque no cumplen los objetivos climÃ¡ticos. Mientras Moreno Bonilla presumÃ­a de la lucha contra el cambio climÃ¡tico, las mÃ¡quinas tractoras arrasan olivos en CÃ³rdoba o JaÃ©n para beneficiar a inversores que nunca han pisado JaÃ©n y que ya han vendido sus derechos en los mercados de futuros.

Pretenden que los europeos paguemos la invasiÃ³n migratoria y todas sus neuras ideolÃ³gicas que solo traen desesperanza, inseguridad, soledad, manadas violentas, escasez de vivienda y colapso de servicios sociales. En resumen, globalismo y propaganda woke, crÃ©dito para las ONG y deuda para los europeos: una pena, un fracaso y una ruina.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Mario Mantovani, a nome del gruppo ECR. â Signor Presidente, signor Commissario, onorevoli colleghi, credo che, quando si parla di Africa, servano rispetto, responsabilitÃ  e visione.Â Dobbiamo porre fine alle politiche predatorie di alcuni governi e affrontare concretamente la questione del debito delle nazioni africane, perchÃ© rafforzare l'Africa significa rafforzare anche l'Europa e costruire insieme una stabilitÃ  comune.

Non sono parole mie, queste, ma di Giorgia Meloni, premier italiano, pronunciate al summit internazionale sul piano Mattei e sono lieto che questa conferenza abbia voluto concretizzare lo spirito con il rilancio della cooperazione, nuove strategie per la sostenibilitÃ  del debito e la realizzazione di progetti e di investimenti concreti.

Per questo la conferenza di Siviglia sarÃ  ricordata e per i 130Â progetti concreti espressi dai governi partecipanti. Tra questi, il piÃ¹ significativo Ã¨ quello del governo italiano, che trasformerÃ , con l'aiuto della finanza innovativa, 230Â milioni di euro di obbligazioni di debito in investimenti concreti.

E per questo voglio ringraziare in modo particolare il viceministro Cirielli, che ha guidato la delegazione del governo italiano alla conferenza, perchÃ© risolvere i problemi dell'Africa significa realizzarne le potenzialitÃ , creare occupazione e benessere dove ora c'Ã¨ povertÃ  e risolvere cosÃ¬ molte delle problematiche europee legate all'immigrazione illegale, alla tratta, allo sfruttamento di esseri umani e al bisogno di sicurezza dei cittadini europei.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Barry Andrews, on behalf of the Renew Group. â Mr President, Commissioner, Minister and colleagues, last week, The Lancet, one of the world's most credible medical journals, published the results of a study into the consequences, as they see it, of US disengagement from overseas development assistance. Their report concluded that US aid disengagement is going to result in an additional 14 million deaths by 2030. We can conclude, even if we believe this is even partly credible, that ODA works. But we know that there is now zero political cost to reducing ODA, whereas there is an enormous human cost, very demonstrably. And this study doesn't even include the impact of EU Member State reductions in overseas development assistance, but we can extrapolate safely that it will probably lead to more than 20 million deaths by 2030.

I'm sure you're also aware of the ODI study that concluded that ODA investment delivers equivalent benefits to the EU economy by, for example, reducing trade barriers. So it works, and it can be part of a principled economic foreign policy. But much of what was agreed at Seville is aspirational. So we have something that works, we have something that demonstrably works and saves lives. So I would implore you, Commissioner, to remain a champion for overseas development assistance. And I warmly welcome your commitment to that in your speech earlier.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Isabella LÃ¶vin, fÃ¶r Verts/ALE gruppen. â Herr talman, herr kommissionÃ¤r! NÃ¥gonting allvarligt hÃ¥ller pÃ¥ att hÃ¤nda med Europas traditionella hÃ¶ger. Vi har alltid tyckt olika, min partigrupp och den konservativa, men en sak har varit given: mÃ¤nskliga rÃ¤ttigheter.

NÃ¤r parlamentet skulle ta fram sin stÃ¥ndpunkt infÃ¶r FN:s bistÃ¥ndskonferens sÃ¥g vi tydliga tecken pÃ¥ att det hÃ¤r hÃ¥ller pÃ¥ att fÃ¶rÃ¤ndras, fÃ¶r dÃ¥ blev en enkel formulering om att fÃ¶retag mÃ¥ste respektera mÃ¤nskliga rÃ¤ttigheter en rÃ¶d linje fÃ¶r Moderaterna och Kristdemokraterna. NÃ¤r jag pÃ¥talar det hÃ¤r i media svarade hÃ¶gern med att det var naivt att tro att de ska acceptera miljÃ¶partistisk bistÃ¥ndspolitik. Men jag undrar: nÃ¤r blev respekten fÃ¶r mÃ¤nskliga rÃ¤ttigheter miljÃ¶partistisk naivitet och nÃ¤r slutade hÃ¶gern stÃ¥ upp fÃ¶r grundlÃ¤ggande rÃ¤ttigheter?

USA:s nedskÃ¤rningar i bistÃ¥ndet fÃ¶rvÃ¤ntas leda till 14Â miljoner fler mÃ¤nniskors dÃ¶d. VÃ¤rldens blickar riktas nu mot EU och vi mÃ¥ste stÃ¥ upp fÃ¶r en solidarisk bistÃ¥ndspolitik.

Â Â Marc Jongen, im Namen der ESN-Fraktion. â Herr PrÃ¤sident, werte Kollegen, Herr Kommissar! Die frÃ¼here Premierministerin des Senegal erklÃ¤rte auf der Konferenz in Sevilla, die EuropÃ¤er seien aufgrund ihrer brutalen Kolonialgeschichte quasi zu ewiger Entwicklungshilfe verpflichtetÂ â im Ãbrigen habe Afrika viele junge Menschen, FachkrÃ¤fte, nach Europa zu exportieren. FÃ¼r mich war das symptomatisch fÃ¼r eine bestimmte Anspruchshaltung dort, die nicht mehr zeitgemÃ¤Ã ist. Herr Bullmann, SPD, der auf demselben Podium saÃ, war so begeistert, dass er die Dame gleich nach BrÃ¼ssel einlud.

Herr Bullmann, werte Kollegen, der Sevilla-Report ist eben darum gescheitert in diesem Haus Ende Juni, weil es genau so nicht weitergehen kann. Die europÃ¤ische Entwicklungspolitik darf nicht auf schlechtem Gewissen gegrÃ¼ndet sein und auch nicht auf Klima- oder Genderideologie, sondern auf geostrategischen und wirtschaftlichen Interessen Europas. Stichwort: Ende der Massenmigration. Wir mÃ¼ssen China ja nicht kopieren, aber etwas mehr Eigeninteresse kÃ¶nnen wir uns von China schon abschauen.

Herr Kommissar SÃ­kela, Sie haben ja einiges Richtige gesagt vorhin, aber rÃ¤umen Sie bitte gleich Trump und Musk in den USA mit Ideologie und Korruption in den EU-FÃ¶rderprogrammen auf. Wir brauchen ein europÃ¤isches DOGE.

Â Â Udo Bullmann (S&D). â Herr PrÃ¤sident, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen, Herr Kommissar, Frau Ministerin! Ich weiÃ nicht, ob in Sevilla alle die ehemalige Ministerin des Senegal richtig verstanden haben: Was sie wollte, war eine echte Partnerschaft. DafÃ¼r hat sie plÃ¤diert, und sie hat uns eingeladen, eine echte Partnerschaft einzugehen. Genau deswegen gehÃ¶ren solche Menschen eingeladen, mit uns darÃ¼ber zu diskutieren, wie das praktisch gehen kann, damit wir einen gemeinsamen Erfolg haben. Das ist meine feste Ãberzeugung. Ich will einfach darauf hinweisen, dass es vor einem Jahr in New York der Globale SÃ¼den war, der mit Europa zusammen das multilaterale System gerettet hat und damit Hoffnungen verknÃ¼pft hat. Wir haben dadurch reagiert, dass wir unsere Entwicklungszusammenarbeit massiv gekÃ¼rzt haben. Herr Kommissar, ich hoffe, Frau Ministerin, ich hoffe, dass von Sevilla mehr Ã¼brig bleibt. Ich will eine Warnung, einen Wunsch Ã¤uÃern an die Adresse von Frau von der Leyen: Wenn sie nÃ¤chste Woche den mehrjÃ¤hrigen Finanzrahmen vorlegt, dann sollte er dafÃ¼r die Gelegenheit bieten und nicht unsere Chancen dadurch verbauen, dass wir nichts mehr messen kÃ¶nnen von dem, was wir gemeinsam unternehmen wollen.

Â Â Tiago Moreira de SÃ¡ (PfE). â Senhor Presidente, no mÃªs passado, o PlenÃ¡rio do Parlamento Europeu rejeitou o relatÃ³rio que visava a ConferÃªncia de Sevilha. Foram 340 eurodeputados contra, 282 a favor. Uma nova maioria polÃ­tica percebeu que aquilo que muitos fingem nÃ£o ver: o modelo atual de financiamento ao desenvolvimento estÃ¡ esgotado. A ConferÃªncia de Sevilha terminou com mais promessas vagas e uma declaraÃ§Ã£o genÃ©rica, o chamado Â«Compromisso de SevilhaÂ», onde se repete tudo e se compromete pouco.

Enquanto isto, o Tribunal de Contas da UniÃ£o Europeia continua a alertar que hÃ¡ graves falhas na forma como os fundos europeus sÃ£o atribuÃ­dos a ONG, muitas vezes sem critÃ©rio, nem consequÃªncias visÃ­veis. Ã preciso coragem para dizer Â«bastaÂ». E ela estÃ¡ a surgir â a ComissÃ£o Europeia jÃ¡ retirou a diretiva das Green Claims, mais um delÃ­rio legislativo ambientalista, sem base cientÃ­fica nem impacto prÃ¡tico.

Estas sÃ£o vitÃ³rias da Direita â conservadora e realista â que defende um novo paradigma: menos ideologia, mais resultados.

Em vez de chorarmos a saÃ­da de cena da USAID, deverÃ­amos seguir o conselho de Margaret Thatcher: Â«NÃ£o sigas a multidÃ£o, deixa a multidÃ£o seguirâteÂ».

A Europa deve liderar â com soluÃ§Ãµes prÃ³prias, parcerias bilaterais sÃ©rias com governos e atores locais, bancos de desenvolvimento multilaterais mais ativos, fundos bem aplicados e foco total em resultados e prestaÃ§Ã£o de contas.

Chega de financiar discursos doutrinÃ¡rios. EstÃ¡ na hora de financiar impacto. EstÃ¡ na hora de exigir responsabilidades e nÃ£o mera retÃ³rica.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Beatrice Timgren (ECR). â Herr talman! Nyligen samlades vÃ¤rldsledare fÃ¶r att utlova det stÃ¶rsta bidragspaketet nÃ¥gonsin till tredje vÃ¤rlden: 4Â biljoner dollar per Ã¥r, med syftet att bekÃ¤mpa den globala fattigdomen. Ambitioner rÃ¥der det ingen brist pÃ¥, men verklighetsfÃ¶rankringen lyser med sin frÃ¥nvaro. TyvÃ¤rr kommer inte det hÃ¤r att hjÃ¤lpa de mest utsatta.

Fokus borde ligga pÃ¥ rent vatten, nÃ¤ring och vÃ¥rd. I stÃ¤llet satsar man pÃ¥ att minska koldioxidutslÃ¤pp, i lÃ¤nder dÃ¤r mÃ¥nga helt saknar el, eller pÃ¥ kvotering och jÃ¤mstÃ¤lldhetsprojekt, medan mammor inte ens kan ge mat till sina barn. Vi har sett det fÃ¶rut. PÃ¥kostade konferenser, fina tal och lÃ¶ften, men sen fÃ¶rsvinner pengarna i byrÃ¥krati och korruption och befÃ¤ster bidragsberoende.

Â Â Charles Goerens (Renew). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, Monsieur le Commissaire, la confÃ©rence de SÃ©ville a Ã©tÃ© lâoccasion de rÃ©pondre aux attentes des personnes qui se retrouvent dans une situation de dÃ©tresse. Malheureusement, les Ãtats-Unis avaient dÃ©jÃ  dÃ©cidÃ© de tourner le dos aux pays qui souffrent en faisant une croix sur 60Â milliards de dollars dâaide publique au dÃ©veloppement.

Sur cette toile de fond, la confÃ©rence de SÃ©ville a Ã©tÃ© lâoccasion de rÃ©pondre, ne fÃ»t-ce que partiellement, aux attentes concernant la famine, lâinsÃ©curitÃ©, la prÃ©caritÃ©, la maladie et le sous-dÃ©veloppement. Le Parlement europÃ©en nâavait hÃ©las pas rÃ©ussi,Â en amont de cette confÃ©rence, Ã  adopter une position susceptible dâinfluer sur le cours des choses. On peut Ã©piloguer longuement sur la question de savoir si, entre-temps, le cÃ´tÃ© indiffÃ©rent ou calculateur de la mouvance trumpiste aura contaminÃ© ce Parlement, entraÃ®nant le rejet de mon rapport.

Toujours est-il que des positions qui avaient Ã©tÃ© rejetÃ©es par le Parlement europÃ©en le mois dernier ont, ce matin, trouvÃ© une trÃ¨s large majoritÃ© en son sein.

Je vous invite dÃ¨s lors Ã  respecter la ligne approuvÃ©e ce matin en matiÃ¨re dâobjectifs de dÃ©veloppement durable et Ã  revenir Ã  la pratique, bien rodÃ©e au sein de cette maison, qui consiste Ã  rechercher des compromis au centre de lâÃ©chiquier politique.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Leire PajÃ­n (S&D). â SeÃ±or presidente, en los Ãºltimos aÃ±os, el nÃºmero de crisis relacionadas con el desarrollo ha aumentado y, sin embargo, han descendido claramente los recursos financieros para hacerles frente.

Cuatro de cada diez personas viven en paÃ­ses donde se destina mÃ¡s dinero a los intereses de la deuda que a la educaciÃ³n pÃºblica o a la salud, como se ha dicho aquÃ­. Y a este ritmo, segÃºn todos los estudios, no cumpliremos ni de lejos los Objetivos de Desarrollo Sostenible.

El mundo nos pide mÃ¡s acciÃ³n y nosotros le damos la espalda. Se la dan los Estados Unidos, sÃ­, pero tambiÃ©n los Estados miembros, que llevan reduciendo la ayuda desde hace mucho tiempo, y tambiÃ©n este Parlamento Europeo, que ni siquiera nos permitiÃ³ tener una posiciÃ³n para Sevilla.

La Cumbre se celebrÃ³ en un paÃ­s europeo por primera vez en muchos aÃ±os. Un paÃ­s que se ha comprometido con el canje de deuda y con la ayuda oficial. Y âcomo decÃ­a el comisarioâ ahora, en este punto de partida de Sevilla, que debe ser para la acciÃ³n, necesitamos avanzar en un sistema financiero global justo, en la cancelaciÃ³n de la deuda, en una arquitectura de salud global mucho mÃ¡s justa.

SeÃ±orÃ­as, este debate no es solo sobre solidaridad, es sobre cÃ³mo construimos el liderazgo polÃ­tico de la UniÃ³n Europea, sobre quÃ© valores, con quÃ© aliados; en definitiva, sobre nuestra talla moral y sobre nuestra credibilidad.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Juan Carlos Girauta Vidal (PfE). â SeÃ±or presidente, esta casa rechazÃ³ por mayorÃ­a estar presente como tal en la Conferencia de Sevilla. Â¿En calidad de quÃ© asistieron los diputados que se presentaron allÃ­?, Â¿en calidad de quÃ©?, Â¿a quiÃ©n representaban?, Â¿no se habrÃ¡n arrogado una representaciÃ³n de la que carecen?

Por otra parte, uno empieza a estar un poco harto de la superioridad moral de una izquierda cuyo modelo de ayuda al desarrollo consiste en financiar a un entramado de ONG, como se ha demostrado cuando, en Estados Unidos, al retirar la financiaciÃ³n de USAID, se ha visto a dÃ³nde iba el dinero. En primer lugar, iba a ONG que lo distribuÃ­an de la manera que les pareciera conveniente y se supeditaban a posicionamientos ideolÃ³gicos, a la ideologÃ­a de gÃ©nero, al catastrofismo climÃ¡tico, etcÃ©tera.

La ayuda al desarrollo es un modelo que, tal como se ha planteado hasta ahora, ha resultado fallido. Lo que desarrolla los paÃ­ses es que se den las condiciones de desarrollo. Estoy citando a Angus Deaton, que es el principal especialista mundial en esta materia, premio nobel de economÃ­a de 2015. Las condiciones de desarrollo son unas instituciones sÃ³lidas, con buena gobernanza y con condiciones de competitividad, con condiciones favorables a los negocios y a la innovaciÃ³n. Cuando estas circunstancias se dan, cuando los Gobiernos son fiables, los jueces independientes, los contratos se cumplen, los registros son fiables..., cuando todo eso ocurre, entonces el desarrollo llega por sÃ­ mismo.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Robert BiedroÅ (S&D). â Mr President, Commissioner, Minister, thank you very much, colleagues, for voting today my and Mr Farantouris' report on SDGs. We go with a strong mandate finally to the UN meeting in New York.

But with five years remaining to implement the 2030 agenda for Sustainable Development, the world is on track to achieve only 17Â % of the targets. We are falling short of trillions of dollars, euros, whatever. We are unfortunately losing a lot of opportunities, and as the UN Secretary-General AntÃ³nio Guterres said, the conference was not about money, it was about investing in the future we want to build together. He was right, because we know there is no plan B for SDGs. That's why we need to seize this opportunity and start implementing policies.

I fully agree with Minister Bjerre: we have 'compass', we have 'pathway' and now we need 'delivery'. This essential for us. This is the obligation, because the promise of SDGs is that no one must be left behind, and we need to deliver.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Murielle Laurent (S&D). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, Monsieur le Commissaire, Madame la Ministre, il y a quelques jours, l'Agence fÃ©dÃ©rale amÃ©ricaine d'aide internationale a cessÃ© d'exister. Selon une Ã©tude rÃ©cente, 14Â millions de personnes pourraient mourir de faim et de maladie d'ici Ã  cinqÂ ans, faute de soutien. Un chiffre effarant. Et pourtant, alors que l'urgence est Ã©vidente, certains Ãtats membres europÃ©ens, dont la France, rÃ©duisent eux aussi leurs engagements.

Au sein de cet hÃ©micycle, le texte prÃ©paratoire Ã  la confÃ©rence de SÃ©ville a Ã©tÃ© rejetÃ© par une alliance dÃ©sormais bien rodÃ©e entre la droite et l'extrÃªme droite. Pendant ce temps, les crises climatiques, sanitaires et gÃ©opolitiques s'accumulent, la famine progresse, la dette qui Ã©trangle les pays les plus pauvres atteint un niveau critique et ce sont les plus vulnÃ©rables, les femmes, les enfants et les rÃ©fugiÃ©s, qui paient le prix fort. Il nous reste cinqÂ ans pour atteindre les objectifs de dÃ©veloppement durable. Alors ne sacrifions pas notre responsabilitÃ© historique et exigeons un budget europÃ©en ambitieux, prÃ©visible et Ã  la hauteur des enjeux.

Â Â Francisco Assis (S&D). â Senhor Presidente, Senhora ComissÃ¡ria, Senhora Ministra, quando os paÃ­ses desenvolvidos ajudam os paÃ­ses em desenvolvimento, o que Ã© uma obrigaÃ§Ã£o moral, tambÃ©m se estÃ£o a ajudar a si mesmos. A pobreza extrema leva Ã s desigualdades, ao Ãªxodo, ao conflito e Ã  guerra. Leva a que esses paÃ­ses fiquem fragilizados no concerto das naÃ§Ãµes, mais vulnerÃ¡veis Ã  influÃªncia estrangeira e Ã  desestabilizaÃ§Ã£o, ao terrorismo, Ã  corrupÃ§Ã£o e Ã s sistemÃ¡ticas violaÃ§Ãµes dos direitos humanos. E isso acaba por ter efeitos negativos tambÃ©m nos paÃ­ses mais prÃ³speros.

Infelizmente, aquilo a que temos assistido Ã© um recuo no apoio ao desenvolvimento, designadamente por parte dos Estados Unidos com o fim do USAID, que pode ter â jÃ¡ estÃ¡ a ter â, de forma cada vez mais visÃ­vel, consequÃªncias trÃ¡gicas para milhÃµes de pessoas. E se Ã© de saudar a enorme convergÃªncia em torno do Compromisso de Sevilha alcanÃ§ado, a retirada das negociaÃ§Ãµes por parte dos Estados Unidos Ã© mais uma confirmaÃ§Ã£o de que Ã© Ã  UniÃ£o Europeia que cabe, neste momento histÃ³rico, estar Ã  altura das suas responsabilidades.

Â Â Joanna Scheuring-Wielgus (S&D). â Panie PrzewodniczÄcy! Panie Komisarzu! Dane, ktÃ³re zostaÅy opublikowane w magazynie The Lancet, sÄ rzeczywiÅcie poraÅ¼ajÄce. 14 milionÃ³w osÃ³b na caÅym Åwiecie moÅ¼e umrzeÄ przez fatalnÄ i zÅÄ politykÄ Donalda Trumpa. Jest to oczywiÅcie wielka tragedia, ale dla nas, dla Europy, jest to naprawdÄ niezwykÅa szansa, Å¼eby stanÄÄ po dobrej stronie historii. MoÅ¼emy siÄ w polityce rozwojowej staÄ liderem, bo my wiemy, Å¼e inwestowanie w globalny rozwÃ³j to inwestycja w przyszÅoÅÄ dla nas wszystkich.

Ja byÅam w Sewilli i chciaÅabym zwrÃ³ciÄ uwagÄ jeszcze na jednÄ rzecz. Podczas obrad braÅam udziaÅ w konferencji o feministycznej polityce zagranicznej i to byÅo bardzo symboliczne, Å¼e byÅo niezwykle duÅ¼e zainteresowanie. W bardzo maÅej sali bardzo duÅ¼o osÃ³b siedziaÅo na podÅodze, rÃ³wnieÅ¼ ja. MÃ³wiÄ o tym dlatego, aby nie ulegaÄ trendom zza oceanu i nie odwracaÄ siÄ od kobiet, bo nawet jeÅ¼eli nie ma miejsca dla nas przy stole, to my i tak wejdziemy drzwiami i oknami i przy tym stole bÄdziemy siedziaÅy. My wiemy, Å¼e bez kobiet nigdy nie osiÄgniemy Å¼adnych celÃ³w, do ktÃ³rych siÄ zobowiÄzaliÅmy. I to jest, uwaÅ¼am, teÅ¼ kluczowe i waÅ¼ne.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Jozef SÃ­kela, Member of the Commission. â Mr President, honourable Members of the European Parliament, in these difficult times for international development cooperation, the fourth international conference in Seville showed that the world can still come together and agree on transformative action. It also showed that Europe is the leader in supporting sustainable development, and this leadership is something we can be proud of. But we must turn this leadership into concrete benefits for our citizens and for our partners.

At the same time, country ownership must make up the bedrock of change. Sustainable development can never be something we do to our partners, but something we can achieve together with them. Multilateralism, international partnerships and effective development cooperation are crucial to bring important support. The commitment of Seville and the 130 initiatives that comprise the Seville platform for action show we are walking the talk.

Dear colleagues, to achieve our ambitions, cooperation across institutions is essential. We should turn Europe's development leadership not only into more global impact, stability or opportunities for our partners, but also into stronger competitiveness and economic security for Europe. This will require closer coordination between the Commission, Member States and this Parliament.

As a former banker and economist, I am used to working with small budgets, with decent budgets, with big budgets. I am used to spending them in a most efficient way, and not overrunning them. What I cannot do is to work without funding I can commit, and without clear balance between authorities and responsibilities. Therefore, let me thank you for today's discussion and I look forward to our further cooperation.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marie Bjerre, President-in-Office of the Council. â MrÂ President, honourable Members, Commissioner, the conference in Seville has set a renewed agenda for financing sustainable development and hope for multilateral cooperation. The EU has played a key role in this by confirming that we are united and reliable. We remain committed to working with all partners to turn the promises of the outcome document into tangible progress, while recognising that much more must be done to address systemic shortcomings and deliver on the SDGs for all. The EU calls for continued ambition, partnership and accountability to ensure that no one is left behind.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â President. â The debate is closed.

Â Â GyÃ¶rgy HÃ¶lvÃ©nyi (PfE), Ã­rÃ¡sban. â A segÃ©ly Ã©s segÃ©lyezett viszonyÃ¡ra Ã©pÃ¼lÅglobÃ¡lis fejlesztÃ©spolitika ideje lejÃ¡rt, fordulÃ³ponthoz Ã©rkeztÃ¼nk. A dÃ¶ntÃ©shozÃ³k most ezt felismerve csÃ¶kkentik az erre szÃ¡nt forrÃ¡sokat. Ez azonban nem jelentheti a szolidaritÃ¡s elvesztÃ©sÃ©t, meg kell talÃ¡lni a helyzetben rejlÅ lehetÅsÃ©get. Most alkalom nyÃ­lik egy olyan fejlesztÃ©si egyÃ¼ttmÅ±kÃ¶dÃ©s kialakÃ­tÃ¡sÃ¡ra, amely vÃ©gre valÃ³ban egyenlÅ partnersÃ©get Ã©pÃ­t. MÃ¡rpedig egyenlÅ partnersÃ©g csak egyenlÅ felelÅssÃ©ggel lehetsÃ©ges. Ideje, hogy partnerorszÃ¡gaink vezetÅi is felelÅssÃ©get vÃ¡llaljanak, Ãºgy politikai, mind pÃ©nzÃ¼gyi szempontbÃ³l. A sevillai konferencia zÃ¡rÃ³dokumentuma helyesen ismeri fel, hogy a biztonsÃ¡g, a megfelelÅ oktatÃ¡s Ã©s szakkÃ©pzÃ©s, valamint a munkahelyek hiÃ¡nya a fejlÅdÃ©s akadÃ¡lyÃ¡t jelenti AfrikÃ¡ban. Â 

18. 51 years after the Turkish invasion of the Republic of Cyprus: condemning the continued Turkish occupation and supporting the resumption of negotiations for a comprehensive solution in line with international law, the UNSC resolutions, EU principles and acquis (debate)
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â PreÅedinte. â UrmÄtorul punct de pe ordinea de zi este dezbaterea privind DeclaraÈia Comisiei referitoare la 51 de ani de la invadarea Republicii Cipru de cÄtre Turcia: condamnarea continuÄrii ocupaÈiei turceÈti Èi sprijinirea reluÄrii negocierilor pentru gÄsirea unei soluÈii cuprinzÄtoare Ã®n conformitate cu dreptul internaÈional, cu rezoluÈiile CSONU Èi cu principiile Èi acquis-ul UE (2025/2794(RSP)).

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Jozef SÃ­kela, Member of the Commission. â Mr President, honourable Members, the European Commission remains fully committed to a comprehensive settlement of the Cyprus issue, and let me be clear what we mean by this: within the UN framework, in accordance with the relevant UN Security Council resolutions and in line with the principles on which the EU is founded and to which it ascribes. These principles include democracy, the rule of law and the right of the people of Cyprus to live in peace without outside interference. These are not empty words. The settlement of the Cyprus issue remains a key priority for the EU.

At the European Council in April 2024, EU leaders emphasised the importance of this issue for the relationship between the EU and TÃ¼rkiye. They made clear that this issue is of particular importance to the EU and an important issue in EU-TÃ¼rkiye relations. Notably, we want to see the resumption of, and progress in, settlement talks to further enhance this relationship. The EU leaders declared their willingness to play an active role in the reunification of Cyprus. The EU stands ready to support all stages of the UN-led process with all appropriate means at its disposal.

The outcome of the informal talks in March was encouraging. The EU strongly supports the work of the UN Secretary-General and his personal envoy in Cyprus, together with the two Cypriot leaders and the three guarantor states. We hope that the next round of such talks scheduled for 16 and 17Â July in New YorkÂ will also produce a positive result.

On 14Â May, the Commission appointed Johannes Hahn, a former European Commissioner, as Special Envoy for Cyprus. He reports directly to the Commission President. His appointment underlines the Commission's commitment to the reunification of Cyprus. Special Envoy Hahn has already travelled to Cyprus. He has had contacts with many actors, including the UN Secretary-General's Personal Envoy in Cyprus, MarÃ­a Angela HolguÃ­n CuÃ©llar. His mandate is to contribute to the settlement process within the UN framework and in close cooperation with the UN Secretary-General's personal envoy.

One of the key issues is the building of trust between the two communities. To facilitate this, the Commission implements major EUâfunded measures in Cyprus. The goal is to build trust between the two communities and so prepare the ground for a resumption of negotiations.

Through the EU aid programme for the Turkish Cypriot community, the European Commission has, since 2026, invested EURÂ 727 million in measures which build trust. We are helping expand trade between the two communities. This leads to more contacts and greater confidence between people. We have offered to help build a biâcommunal solar power plant. This would increase biâcommunal cooperation in the energy field. We are implementing the allâisland halloumi/hellim protected designation of origin scheme in Cyprus. This protects a centuriesâold product of the whole island, bringing together and benefiting both Greek Cypriot and Turkish Cypriot producers.

EU funding is also bringing together civil society organisations from the two communities in fields such as education, environment and culture. EU funding is restoring cultural monuments across the island, conserving Cyprus's rich and multifaceted cultural heritage. On a more sombre note, EU funding is helping find the remains of people who went missing during the fighting in 1963, 1964 and 1974. This is a reminder of the human cost of the conflict, and should spur us all on to find a peaceful solution, supporting Cypriot reunification within the UN framework.

The EU will continue to invest political and financial capital in this solution, supporting dialogue and cooperation, implementing confidenceâbuilding measures and paving the way for formal settlement negotiations. This issue is made all the more pressing by today's geopolitical shifts. In these troubled times, we must do all we can to promote stability and security in the Eastern Mediterranean and beyond. Thank you very much. I'm looking forward to our discussion today.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

ÎÏÏÏ ÏÎ¿Ï ÏÎµÎ»ÎµÎ¹ÏÎ½ÎµÎ¹ ÎµÎ¯Î½Î±Î¹ Î· ÏÏÎ¿Î¼Î¿Î½Î® ÎºÎ±Î¹ Î· Î±Î½Î¿ÏÎ® Î¼Î±Ï, Î´Î¹ÏÏÎ¹ Î±ÏÏÏ ÏÎ¿Ï ÏÏÎ¼Î²Î±Î¯Î½ÎµÎ¹ ÎµÎ¯Î½Î±Î¹ ÏÏÎ½ÎµÎ½Î¿ÏÎ®. ÎÎ± Î¼ÏÎ¿ÏÎ¿ÏÏÎ± Î½Î± ÎµÎºÏÏÎ¬ÏÏ Î¹ÎºÎ±Î½Î¿ÏÎ¿Î¯Î·ÏÎ· ÏÎ¿Ï ÏÎ¹Î¿Î¸ÎµÏÎ®Î¸Î·ÎºÎµ ÏÎ¿ÏÎ»Î¬ÏÎ¹ÏÏÎ¿ ÏÎ¿ Î±Î¯ÏÎ·Î¼Î¬ Î¼Î¿Ï Î³Î¹Î± ÏÏÎ¶Î®ÏÎ·ÏÎ·. ÎÏÏÎ¹, Î±Î½ÏÎ¯ Î½Î± ÏÏÎ¿ÏÎ¸Î¿ÏÎ½, ÏÏÎ¿Î½ÏÎ¼ÎµÏÏÎ±Î½ ÏÎ¿ ÎºÎ±ÏÎ±Î´Î¹ÎºÎ±ÏÏÎ¹ÎºÏ ÏÎ®ÏÎ¹ÏÎ¼Î± ÎºÎ±ÏÎ¬ ÏÎ·Ï Î¤Î¿ÏÏÎºÎ¯Î±Ï.

Â Â Kai Tegethoff, on behalf of the Verts/ALE Group. â MrÂ President,Â 51 years of Turkish troops on EU ground is shattering trauma for Cyprus. For half a century, the island and its people have remained divided. A European capital cut in half.

But the deeper tragedy is that generation after generation of Cypriots have been let down. Let down by leaderships that lack courage, let down by politicians who preferred nationalism over compromise and let down by a world that treats the status quo as acceptable.Â Europe cannot claim to defend international law while tolerating its violation in Cyprus.

If we want peace, justice and European unity, we must act with clarity, urgency and political will. Pick up the threat from 2017 and work towards a peaceful, united, free and independent federal future. Cyprus deserves much more than commemoration.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â ÎÎ¹ÏÏÎ³Î¿Ï ÎÎµÏÏÎ³Î¯Î¿Ï, ÎµÎ¾ Î¿Î½ÏÎ¼Î±ÏÎ¿Ï ÏÎ·Ï Î¿Î¼Î¬Î´Î±Ï The Left. â ÎÏÏÎ¹Îµ Î ÏÏÎµÎ´ÏÎµ, ÎºÏÏÎ¹Îµ ÎÏÎ¯ÏÏÎ¿ÏÎµ, Î­Î½Î± ÏÎ±ÏÎ¹ÏÏÎ¹ÎºÏ ÏÏÎ±Î¾Î¹ÎºÏÏÎ·Î¼Î± ÎºÎ±Î¹ Î¼Î¹Î± ÎµÎ¹ÏÎ²Î¿Î»Î® Î±ÏÏ ÏÎ·Î½ Î¤Î¿ÏÏÎºÎ¯Î±, ÏÎ»Î± Î¼ÎµÎ¸Î¿Î´ÎµÏÎ¼Î­Î½Î± Î±ÏÏ ÏÎ¿ ÎÎÎ¤Î Î­ÏÎµÏÎ±Î½ ÏÏÎ¿Î½ ÏÏÏÎ¿ Î¼Î¿Ï ÏÎ·Î½ Î´Î¹ÏÎ¿ÏÏÎ¼Î·ÏÎ·, Î¿ÏÏÎ¹Î±ÏÏÎ¹ÎºÎ¬. 51 ÏÏÏÎ½Î¹Î± Î¼ÎµÏÎ¬, Î±ÏÏÎ® Î· Î´Î¹ÏÎ¿ÏÏÎ¼Î·ÏÎ· Î¼Îµ Î­Î½Î±Î½ ÎµÏÎ¹Î±Î»ÏÎ¹ÎºÏ ÏÏÏÏÎ¿ ÎµÎ¯Î½Î±Î¹ Î¼ÏÏÎ¿ÏÏÎ¬ Î¼Î±Ï. ÎÎ»Î»Î¹ÏÏ, ÏÎ¹ Î¼Î­Î½ÎµÎ¹; ÎÎ­Î½ÎµÎ¹ Î¼Î¹Î± ÎÏÏÏÎ¿Ï Î´Î¹ÏÎ¿ÏÎ¿Î¼Î·Î¼Î­Î½Î·, ÏÏ Î±Î¹ÏÎ½Î¹Î¿ ÏÏÎ¯Î³Î¼Î± Î½ÏÏÎ¿ÏÎ®Ï Î³Î¹Î± ÏÎ·Î½ Î¯Î´Î¹Î± ÏÎ·Î½ ÎÎ½ÏÏÎ· Î±Î»Î»Î¬ ÎºÎ±Î¹ Î³Î¹Î± ÏÎ· Î´Î¹ÎµÎ¸Î½Î® ÎºÎ¿Î¹Î½ÏÏÎ·ÏÎ±. ÎÎ± ÏÎ¿ Î´ÎµÏÏÎµÎ¯ÏÎµ; ÎÎ¼ÎµÎ¯Ï, ÏÎ±Ï Î´Î¹Î±Î²ÎµÎ²Î±Î¹Ï, ÏÏ ÎÏÎ¹ÏÏÎµÏÎ¬, ÎµÎ´Ï ÎºÎ±Î¹ ÏÏÎ·Î½ ÎÏÏÏÎ¿, Î´ÎµÎ½ Î¸Î± ÏÎ¿ Î´ÎµÏÏÎ¿ÏÎ¼Îµ ÏÎ¿ÏÎ­.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marc Jongen, on behalf of the ESN Group. â Mr President, in the 21st century in Europe, there is still a divided country: Cyprus. And that's a disgrace, dear colleagues. As in any such longâlasting conflict, there is great mistrust and hurt on both sides, the Greek and the Turkish. Anyone who saw the interesting and courageous interview by our colleague FidiasÂ Panayiotou with the political leader of Turkish Northern Cyprus will understand that there is no simple solution, but there can be a solution to this conflict.

Whether a confederation orÂ a return to a unitary state is the best option, it only be resolved diplomatically between Greek and Turkish Cypriots, but the EU can provide and must provide much more support here, including more pressure on TÃ¼rkiye, not least because a lot of illegal migration to Europe passes through Cyprus from the north to the south. And this has to stop.

One thing is also clear: TÃ¼rkiye's role in the Cyprus conflict is one more reason to end the EU accession negotiations with TÃ¼rkiye once and for all, and to finally stop the billions in payments to TÃ¼rkiye that are still being made in this context.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Jozef SÃ­kela, Member of the Commission. â Mr President, many thanks to all who have spoken. The Commission takes note of your contributions to this very important topic. In an unstable world, it becomes more important than ever that we stay true to our European values, including peaceful coexistence, democracy and the rule of law. We seek a successful settlement of the Cyprus issue, knowing that it will be highly beneficial not just for the people of Cyprus, not only for the Eastern Mediterranean, but for the whole of Europe.

A successful reunification, with the peaceful integration of all Cypriots under the United Nations-led process will have many lessons applicable elsewhere. We in the European Commission wholeheartedly support this process and will continue to do so every step of the way.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â President. â The debate is closed.

DeclaraÈii scrise (articolul 178)

Î Î¤Î¿ÏÏÎºÎ¯Î± ÎºÎ±ÏÎ­ÏÎµÎ¹ Î±ÏÏ ÏÎ¿ 1974 ÏÎ¿ 37% ÏÎ·Ï ÎÏÏÏÎ¹Î±ÎºÎ®Ï ÎÎ·Î¼Î¿ÎºÏÎ±ÏÎ¯Î±Ï ÎºÎ±Î¹ Î­ÏÎµÎ¹ ÎµÎ³ÎºÎ±ÏÎ±ÏÏÎ®ÏÎµÎ¹ ÏÎ¬Î½Ï Î±ÏÏ 300.000 ÎµÏÎ¿Î¯ÎºÎ¿ÏÏ, Î±Î»Î»Î¿Î¹ÏÎ½Î¿Î½ÏÎ±Ï ÏÎ¿Î½ ÏÎ»Î·Î¸ÏÏÎ¼Ï. ÎÎµÎ½ ÏÏÎ¬ÏÏÎµÎ¹ ÏÎ»Î­Î¿Î½ ÏÎ¿Î»Î¹ÏÎ¹ÎºÎ® Î²Î¬ÏÎ· Î³Î¹Î± ÏÎ¿Î»Î¹ÏÎ¹ÎºÎ® Î¹ÏÏÏÎ·ÏÎ± ÏÎµ ÎµÏÎ¯ÏÎµÎ´Î¿ ÎºÎµÎ½ÏÏÎ¹ÎºÎ®Ï ÎµÎ¾Î¿ÏÏÎ¯Î±Ï.

Î ÎÎ ÎºÎ±Î»ÏÏÏÎµÎ¹ ÏÎ¹Ï ÎµÏÎ¸ÏÎ½ÎµÏ ÏÎ·Ï, ÎµÏÎ¹ÎºÎ±Î»Î¿ÏÎ¼ÎµÎ½Î· Î´Î¹Î±ÏÏÎ±Î³Î¼Î±ÏÎµÏÏÎµÎ¹Ï ÏÏÏ ÏÎ¿Î½ ÎÎÎ.
